content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand my task as the lean-auditor subagent.
I'll start by reading the instruction files and then audit the relevant Lean files.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
162

9	/-!
10	# Slice transport for the dual (A.1.c.SubT §Dual SliceTransport)
11	
12	Auxiliary declarations feeding `DualInverse.lean`:
13	- §0: `unitDualSectionEquiv`, `dualUnitIsoGen` (`namespace PresheafOfModules`)
14	- §A: `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`, `dualUnitRingSwap`, `dualUnitRingSwapInv`,
15	  `dualUnitRingSwapHom`, `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso_hom`,
16	  `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_presheafMap`, `unitRelabelSwap`,
17	  `sliceDualTransportInv`, `sliceDualTransport` (`namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules`)
18	-/
19	set_option autoImplicit false
20	
21	universe u
22	
23	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory
24	
25	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
26	
27	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
28	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
29	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
30	
31	namespace PresheafOfModules
32	
33	open InternalHom Opposite
34	
35	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
36	
37	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
38	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
39	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
40	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
41	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
42	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
43	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
44	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
45	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
46	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
47	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
48	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
49	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
50	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
51	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
52	  exact
53	    { toFun := fun φ =>
54	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
55	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
56	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
57	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
58	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
59	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
60	      invFun := fun r =>
61	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
62	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
63	      left_inv := fun φ => by
64	        ext Y
65	        dsimp only
66	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
67	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
68	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
69	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
70	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
71	        rfl
72	      right_inv := fun r => by
73	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
74	            (restr X.unop
75	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
76	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
77	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
78	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
79	        exact mul_one r }
80	
81	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
82	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
83	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
84	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
85	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
86	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
87	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
88	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
89	    (fun {X Y} f => by
90	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
91	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
92	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
93	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
94	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
95	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
96	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
97	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
98	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
99	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
100	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
101	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
102	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
103	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
104	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
105	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
106	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
107	      rw [hrm] at key
108	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
109	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
110	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
111	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
112	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
113	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
114	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
115	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
116	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
117	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
118	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
119	
120	end PresheafOfModules

128	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
129	
130	open Opposite in
131	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion
132	structure ring iso `(f.appIso W').inv` is an isomorphism.**  Its underlying map is the (bijective)
133	ring map `(f.appIso W').inv.hom`, so `ε` is an iso by `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`
134	(`PresheafInternalHom.lean`) fed the bijectivity from `ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso`.  This
135	is the single load-bearing fact powering `dualUnitRingSwap` (the codomain unit ring swap of leg-B),
136	phrased at the `CommRingCat` carrier so `CommRing` is native (per `analogies/ma-legb262.md`). -/
137	lemma isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
138	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
139	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
140	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom)) :=
141	  restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom
142	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv)
143	
144	open Opposite in
145	/-- **Leg-B: the codomain unit ring-iso swap** `restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW')) ⟶
146	𝟙_Y(W')`.  It is the inverse of the lax-monoidal unit `ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)`,
147	an isomorphism by `isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso`.  The endpoints are written at the canonical
148	`CommRingCat` section carriers `↑(X.presheaf.obj _)` / `↑(Y.presheaf.obj _)` (the `forget₂`-composite
149	carrier breaks `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis, `analogies/ma-legb262.md`); they reconcile by
150	`rfl`/defeq with the `restr`/`𝟙_`-section spellings of `sliceDualTransport`'s `codomainMap` hole. -/
151	noncomputable def dualUnitRingSwap {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
152	    (W' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y) :
153	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom).obj
154	        (𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(X.presheaf.obj (op ((Scheme.Hom.opensFunctor f).obj W'))))) ⟶
155	      𝟙_ (ModuleCat ↑(Y.presheaf.obj (op W'))) :=
156	  haveI := isIso_ε_restrictScalars_appIso f W'
157	  CategoryTheory.inv (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε
158	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.Hom.appIso f W').inv.hom))
159	
160	open Opposite in
161	/-- **Leg-B (inverse direction): the unit codomain ring-iso swap for `invFun`** `𝟙_Y(W') ⟶
162	restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv (𝟙_X(fW'))`.  This is the lax-monoidal unit
163	`ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso W').inv.hom)` ITSELF (not its inverse), the reverse of

358	    -- so post-composing it with `(f.appIso P).hom` is the identity on `𝒪_X(f''ᵁP)`.  This is the
359	    -- load-bearing ring identity that collapses the double `restrictScalars` in the reverse
360	    -- component (`?collapse`); it is FALSE for an arbitrary `β`, hence supplied as a hypothesis and
361	    -- discharged at the unique caller (`sliceDualTransport.invFun`) via `Iso.hom_inv_id`.

486	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
487	of `dual_restrict_iso`:

489	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
490	```

492	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).

495	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
496	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
497	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
498	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/

505	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V) ≅

697	  --     `f.opensFunctor.obj P = W''.left` by `image_preimage_of_le (..) W''.hom.le`).  The component
698	  --     at `W''` is the X-slice mirror of `toFun`:
699	  --       eqToHom (M.val.map: M.val(op W''.left) ≅ M.val(op fP), from image_preimage_of_le) ≫
700	  --       (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom).map (ψ.app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hPV)))) ≫
701	  --       dualUnitRingSwapHom f P                                         -- codomain swap = `inv ε`,
702	  --                                                                       -- the `.hom`-direction
703	  --     all conjugated by the `eqToHom`s from `image_preimage_of_le` (mirror of `homLocalSection`).
704	  --     NOTE (direction fix, supersedes the prior "ε itself not inv ε" gloss): the codomain swap is
705	  --     `dualUnitRingSwapHom = inv (ε (restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom))` — i.e. `inv ε` of the
706	  --     `.hom`-direction functor, because the reindex now uses `restrictScalars (f.appIso P).hom.hom`
707	  --     (the `.hom`, not `.inv`, since we transport a `𝒪_Y(P)`-section map back to a `𝒪_X(fP)`-map).
708	  --     `map_add'`/`map_smul'` of this reverse map mirror the closed forward proofs (refine_2/3
709	  --     templates); naturality is the thin-poset `Subsingleton.elim` + ε-naturality square.
710	  --     STATUS (iter-271): the reverse map is now the EXTRACTED top-level def
711	  --     `sliceDualTransportInv f M V β` (the binder-metavar unstick lever); its `app`/`naturality`
712	  --     remain the documented residuals there.  `invFun` is wired to it below.
713	  · refine fun ψ => sliceDualTransportInv f M V β ?_ ψ
714	    -- Discharge the β-compatibility hypothesis for the specific `β = whiskerRight (f.appIso).inv`:
715	    -- `(β.app (op P)).hom = (f.appIso P).inv.hom`, so the composite with `(f.appIso P).hom` is the
716	    -- identity by `Iso.hom_inv_id` of the structure ring iso.
717	    intro P
718	    rw [hβ]
719	    have h := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom_inv_id
720	    simpa only [Functor.whiskerRight_app, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_map_hom,
721	      CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_id] using h
722	  -- (5) left_inv: `invFun (toFun φ) = φ`, collapses via `Iso.inv_hom_id` of `f.appIso`
723	  --     (`dualUnitRingSwap`/`ε` round-trip) + the down-set bijection.  Blocked on (4).
724	  · sorry -- REPAIR: sorry inserted at broken proof site; fill via analogies/dualnat006.md
725	  -- (6) right_inv: `toFun (invFun ψ) = ψ`, the `Iso.hom_inv_id` mirror of (5).  Blocked on (4).
726	  · sorry -- REPAIR: sorry inserted at broken proof site; fill via analogies/dualnat006.md

9	/-!
10	# Vestigial / quarantine sub-module of `TensorObjSubstrate`
11	
12	This file quarantines three sections of the substrate build that are NOT on the
13	primary `exists_tensorObj_inverse` closure path but may serve future prover work:
14	
15	- `FlatWhisker` / `WhiskerOfW` (route-(e) flatness-free whiskering; one open sorry
16	  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`)
17	- `StalkLinearMap` (route-(e) d.1 ingredient; axiom-clean)
18	- `OverSliceSheafEquiv` (the shared root for both ⊗-inverse bridges; axiom-clean)
19	
20	Imported by `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate` (the public API file).
21	-/
22	
23	set_option autoImplicit false
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory
28	
29	namespace PresheafOfModules
30	
31	universe v'
32	
33	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
34	
35	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
36	sheafification localizer
37	
38	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
39	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
40	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
41	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
42	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
43	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
44	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
45	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
46	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
47	structure. -/
48	
49	section FlatWhisker
50	
51	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
52	
53	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
54	
55	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
56	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
57	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
58	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
59	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
60	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
61	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
62	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
63	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
64	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
65	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
66	
67	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
68	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
69	half. -/
70	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
71	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
72	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
73	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
74	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
75	  constructor
76	  intro U s
77	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
78	  | zero =>
79	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
80	      intro V i _
81	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
82	  | tmul a b =>
83	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
84	      intro V i hi
85	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
86	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
87	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
88	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
89	      congr 1
90	  | add s t hs ht =>
91	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
92	      intro V i hi
93	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
94	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
95	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
96	
97	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
98	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
99	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
100	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
101	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
102	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
103	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
104	read at a single morphism. -/
105	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
106	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
107	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
108	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
109	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
110	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
111	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
112	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
113	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
114	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
115	  exact h2
116	
117	end FlatWhisker
118	
119	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
120	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
121	
122	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
123	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
124	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
125	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
126	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
127	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
128	
129	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
130	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
131	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
132	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
133	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
134	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
135	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
136	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
137	
138	section StalkLinearMap
139	
140	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
141	
142	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
143	
144	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
145	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
146	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
147	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
148	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
149	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
150	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
151	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
152	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
153	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
154	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
155	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
156	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
157	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
158	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
159	  map_smul' r s := by
160	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
161	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
162	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
163	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
164	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
165	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
166	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
167	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
168	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
169	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
170	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
171	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
172	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
173	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
174	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
175	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
176	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
177	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
178	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
179	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
180	      intro z
181	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
182	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
183	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
184	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
185	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
186	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
187	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
188	
189	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
190	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
191	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
192	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
193	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
194	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
195	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
196	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
197	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
198	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
199	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
200	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
201	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
202	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
203	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
204	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
205	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
206	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
207	
208	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
209	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
210	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
211	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
212	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
213	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
214	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
215	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
216	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
217	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
218	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
219	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
220	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
221	
222	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
223	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
224	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
225	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
226	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
227	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
228	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
229	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
230	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
231	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
232	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
233	
234	end StalkLinearMap
235	
236	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — stalk bridges for the topological site
237	
238	For the topological site `Opens X`, a morphism of presheaves valued in a concrete
239	category is locally injective iff it is injective on all stalks. Mathlib supplies the
240	*surjective* analogue for presheaves (`locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks`) and
241	the *injective* one only at the sheaf level (`app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`);
242	the presheaf-level local-injectivity bridge is project-local. Combined, they characterise
243	the sheafification localizer `J.W` stalkwise (`isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W`), the (d.1)
244	ingredient of the route-(d) whiskering closure. -/
245	
246	section StalkBridge
247	
248	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
249	
250	universe w
251	
252	variable {A : Type w} [Category.{u} A] [Limits.HasColimits A]
253	  {FA : A → A → Type*} {CA : A → Type u} [(P Q : A) → FunLike (FA P Q) (CA P) (CA Q)]
254	  [ConcreteCategory A FA] [Limits.PreservesFilteredColimits (forget A)]
255	  {Z : TopCat.{u}}
256	
257	/-- **Injective on stalks ⇒ locally injective (topological site).** For a morphism `T` of
258	presheaves valued in a concrete category over a topological space `Z`, stalkwise injectivity
259	implies local injectivity for `Opens.grothendieckTopology Z`. Project-local: Mathlib provides
260	this only at the sheaf level (`app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`); here it is the
261	presheaf-level statement, proved directly through `germ_eq` (the equalizer sieve covers each
262	point because agreeing germs agree on a neighbourhood). -/
263	theorem isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk
264	    {F G : TopCat.Presheaf A Z} (T : F ⟶ G)
265	    (h : ∀ z : Z, Function.Injective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((stalkFunctor A z).map T))) :
266	    CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) T := by
267	  refine ⟨fun {U'} s t hst z hz => ?_⟩
268	  have hgerm : (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ (unop U') z hz)) s
269	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ (unop U') z hz)) t := by
270	    apply h z
271	    rw [stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply, stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply, hst]
272	  obtain ⟨W, hzW, iU, iV, heq⟩ := F.germ_eq z hz hz s t hgerm
273	  refine ⟨W, iU, ?_, hzW⟩
274	  rw [Subsingleton.elim iV iU] at heq
275	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply]
276	  exact heq
277	
278	/-- **Locally injective ⇒ injective on stalks (topological site).** The converse of
279	`isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk`: a locally injective morphism of presheaves over a
280	topological space induces injective stalk maps. Proved via `germ_eq` and the covering of the
281	equalizer sieve. -/
282	theorem injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective
283	    {F G : TopCat.Presheaf A Z} (T : F ⟶ G)
284	    (hT : CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) T)
285	    (z : Z) :
286	    Function.Injective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((stalkFunctor A z).map T)) := by
287	  intro sₓ tₓ hxeq
288	  obtain ⟨U, hzU, s, rfl⟩ := F.germ_exist z sₓ
289	  obtain ⟨V, hzV, t, rfl⟩ := F.germ_exist z tₓ
290	  rw [stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply, stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply] at hxeq
291	  obtain ⟨W, hzW, iU, iV, heqG⟩ := G.germ_eq z hzU hzV
292	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op U))) s) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op V))) t) hxeq
293	  set a := (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.map iU.op)) s with ha
294	  set b := (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.map iV.op)) t with hb
295	  have hTab : (ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op W))) a
296	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (T.app (op W))) b := by
297	    rw [ha, hb, NatTrans.naturality_apply T iU.op s, NatTrans.naturality_apply T iV.op t]
298	    exact heqG
299	  obtain ⟨W', f, hf, hzW'⟩ := hT.equalizerSieve_mem (X := op W) a b hTab z hzW
300	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf
301	  have e3 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ W z hzW)) a
302	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.germ W z hzW)) b := by
303	    rw [← F.germ_res_apply f z hzW' a, ← F.germ_res_apply f z hzW' b, hf]
304	  rw [← F.germ_res_apply iU z hzW s, ← F.germ_res_apply iV z hzW t, ← ha, ← hb, e3]
305	
306	/-- **The sheafification localizer is a stalkwise isomorphism (topological site).** For
307	presheaves of abelian groups on a topological space `Z` whose Grothendieck topology has
308	`WEqualsLocallyBijective`, a morphism in the sheafification localizer `J.W` induces an
309	isomorphism on every stalk. This is the (d.1) bridge: it combines the injective stalk bridge
310	`injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective` with Mathlib's surjective one
311	`locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks`, and `ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective`. -/
312	theorem isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W {Z : TopCat.{u}}
313	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology Z).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
314	    {F G : TopCat.Presheaf AddCommGrpCat.{u} Z} (T : F ⟶ G)
315	    (hT : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z).W T) (z : Z) :
316	    IsIso ((stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} z).map T) := by
317	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hT
318	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
319	  exact ⟨injective_stalk_of_isLocallyInjective T hT.1 z,
320	    (TopCat.Presheaf.locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks T).mp hT.2 z⟩
321	
322	end StalkBridge
323	
324	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
325	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), realized via the stalk–tensor commutation d.2)
326	
327	The flat whiskering `W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat` needs the SECTIONWISE flatness instance
328	`∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible sheaves over non-affine opens
329	and is therefore OFF the associator critical path. The associator only ever whiskers the
330	sheafification UNIT `η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`). Whiskering a
331	*locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves local bijectivity with NO flatness
332	hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism
333	(d.1, `isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W`), the tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism by the
334	stalk–tensor commutation (d.2, `stalkTensorIso`) — flatness-free, because *isomorphisms*
335	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. -/
336	
337	section WhiskerOfW
338	
339	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
340	open PresheafOfModules.Monoidal
341	
342	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
343	
344	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity (flatness-free).**
345	For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g` whose underlying additive map is
346	locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective. The
347	proof is the stalkwise computation `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: by (d.1) `g_x` is an
348	isomorphism, and by the (d.2) stalk–tensor commutation `stalkTensorIso` the stalk of `F ◁ g`
349	is identified with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`, hence an isomorphism — for *any* `F`, with neither
350	sectionwise flatness nor local triviality of `F`. This is the single residual ingredient of the
351	associator `tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). -/
352	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W
353	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
354	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
355	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
356	    (hg : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
357	    IsLocallyInjective (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (F ◁ g) := by
358	  -- (d.1) `g` is a stalkwise isomorphism.
359	  have hgx : ∀ z : X, IsIso ((stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} z).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :=
360	    fun z => isIso_stalkFunctor_map_of_W _ hg z
361	  -- Reduce to stalkwise injectivity of `F ◁ g`.
362	  apply isLocallyInjective_of_injective_stalk
363	  intro z
364	  -- The (d.2) stalk–tensor commutation isomorphisms and the stalkwise iso of `g`.
365	  set eM := stalkTensorIso F M z with heM
366	  set eN := stalkTensorIso F N z with heN
367	  set e := stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso g z (hgx z) with he
368	  set E := TensorProduct.congr
369	    (LinearEquiv.refl (↑(R.stalk z)) (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk F.presheaf z))) e with hE
370	  set φ := (stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} z).map ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) with hφ
371	  -- B-naturality: under `eM`/`eN`, the stalk of `F ◁ g` is `id_{F_z} ⊗ g_z`.
372	  have key : ∀ w : ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf z),
373	      eN (ConcreteCategory.hom φ w) = E (eM w) := by
374	    intro w
375	    obtain ⟨W, hzW, p, rfl⟩ :=
376	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf z w
377	    -- The germ map, `φ`, `eN`, `eM`, `E` are all additive; the `+`/`0` instances on the
378	    -- defeq carriers `(toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)` vs `(Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf` differ
379	    -- syntactically, so we distribute `map_add`/`map_zero` via `have`-terms (defeq-coerced).
380	    induction p using TensorProduct.induction_on with
381	    | zero =>
382	        erw [map_zero (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)),
383	          map_zero (ConcreteCategory.hom φ), map_zero eN, map_zero eM, map_zero E]
384	    | tmul a m =>
385	        -- RHS: `eM (germ (a ⊗ m)) = germ_F a ⊗ germ_M m`, then `E` sends it to
386	        -- `germ_F a ⊗ e (germ_M m) = germ_F a ⊗ germ_N (g_W m)`.
387	        have hRM : eM ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW))
388	              (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))] m))
389	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ F.presheaf W z hzW)) a
390	              ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk z)]
391	                (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W z hzW)) m :=
392	          stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul F M z W hzW a m
393	        have hLN : eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F N).presheaf W z hzW))
394	              (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))]
395	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m)))
396	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ F.presheaf W z hzW)) a
397	              ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk z)]
398	                (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W z hzW))
399	                  ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m) :=
400	          stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul F N z W hzW a ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m)
401	        have hL1 : (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
402	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW))
403	                (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))] m))
404	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F N).presheaf W z hzW))
405	                (a ⊗ₜ[↑((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj (op W))]
406	                  ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) m)) := by
407	          erw [stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]
408	          congr 1
409	        rw [hL1, hLN, hRM, hE, TensorProduct.congr_tmul, LinearEquiv.refl_apply, he]
410	        congr 1
411	        exact (stalkLinearMap_germ g z W hzW m).symm
412	    | add p q hp hq =>
413	        have hg2 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) (p + q)
414	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p
415	              + (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q :=
416	          map_add _ _ _
417	        rw [hg2]
418	        have hphi2 : (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
419	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p
420	                + (ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q)
421	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
422	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p)
423	              + (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
424	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q) :=
425	          map_add _ _ _
426	        rw [hphi2]
427	        have heN2 : eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
428	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p)
429	            + (ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
430	              ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q))
431	            = eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
432	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) p))
433	              + eN ((ConcreteCategory.hom φ)
434	                ((ConcreteCategory.hom (germ (Monoidal.tensorObj F M).presheaf W z hzW)) q)) :=
435	          map_add _ _ _
436	        rw [heN2]
437	        simp only [map_add]
438	        rw [hp, hq]
439	  -- Hence the stalk map of `F ◁ g` is a composite of bijections.
440	  have hbij : Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom φ) := by
441	    have hfun : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom φ) = ⇑eN.symm ∘ ⇑E ∘ ⇑eM := by
442	      funext w
443	      rw [Function.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, ← key w, eN.symm_apply_apply]
444	    rw [hfun]
445	    exact eN.symm.bijective.comp (E.bijective.comp eM.bijective)
446	  exact hbij.1
447	
448	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The ROUTE (d)
449	replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a locally bijective `g`
450	(`∈ J.W`), `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`. Local surjectivity is free
451	(`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness); local injectivity is the flatness-free
452	stalkwise residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
453	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W
454	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
455	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
456	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
457	    (hg : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
458	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
459	  have hbij := hg
460	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
461	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
462	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
463	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
464	
465	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
466	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
467	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W
468	    [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
469	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
470	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
471	    (hg : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
472	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
473	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
474	  have hconj : g ▷ F
475	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
476	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
477	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
478	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
479	  rw [(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W.cancel_left_of_respectsIso,
480	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W.cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
481	  exact hwl
482	
483	end WhiskerOfW
484	
485	end PresheafOfModules

487	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the open-immersion↔slice sheaf-site
488	equivalence (the SHARED root of both ⊗-inverse bridges)
489	
490	For a topological space `X` and an open `U : Opens X`, Mathlib supplies the
491	1-categorical equivalence `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
492	(`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`) but leaves, as a documented `## TODO`, the
493	upgrade of this to an *equivalence of sheaf categories*
494	
495	  `Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
496	
497	This is the single Mathlib-absent root on which BOTH remaining ⊗-inverse bridges
498	(the A-engine `homOfLocalCompat` gluing and the C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`)
499	reduce: re-evaluating a slice-internal-hom along the open immersion `U ↪ X` is
500	exactly transport across this sheaf-site equivalence. It is value-category
501	parametric (`A` is arbitrary), so one build serves both lanes.
502	
503	The proof routes through `CategoryTheory.Equivalence.sheafCongr`, which needs only
504	the dense-subsite instance `(overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite …`. The sole
505	non-formal content of that instance is the cover-correspondence
506	`functorPushforward_mem_iff`, which we discharge *pointwise*: on the thin poset
507	`Opens X` a covering sieve is a pointwise neighbourhood cover, and the open
508	embedding `↥U ↪ X` matches points and opens on both sides. No `Over.map`
509	pseudofunctor coherence appears — thinness trivialises it.
510	
511	Per blueprint `lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv`. -/
512	
513	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
514	
515	section OverSliceSheafEquiv
516	
517	open Topology CategoryTheory
518	
519	universe v w
520	
521	-- NOTE: within the `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` namespace the unqualified
522	-- identifier `Opens` resolves to the scheme-theoretic `Scheme.Opens` (expecting a
523	-- `Scheme`), shadowing the intended point-set `TopologicalSpace.Opens`. We therefore
524	-- fully qualify every `TopologicalSpace.Opens …` here; `Over`/`Sieve`/`GrothendieckTopology`
525	-- are `CategoryTheory` names with no scheme shadow and stay unqualified.
526	
527	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
528	
529	end OverSliceSheafEquiv
530	
531	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

172	noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
173	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
174	    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by
175	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
176	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
177	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
178	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
179	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
180	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
181	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
182	      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
183	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal
184	  --   `(pullback φ).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((M.restrict f).val)`.
185	  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
186	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
187	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
188	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
189	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
190	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
191	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
192	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
193	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
194	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
195	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
196	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
197	  -- Residual: `(pushforward β).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`.
198	  -- Assemble sectionwise from `sliceDualTransport`.  The `isoMk` naturality square is the
199	  -- thin-poset `Opens Y` coherence of the `sliceDualTransport` family; it becomes routine once
200	  -- `sliceDualTransport`'s body is concrete (its `.hom` is currently a `sorry`, so the square
201	  -- cannot be discharged yet — it is left as the assembly residual, per the planner bar).
202	  refine PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun V => sliceDualTransport f M V) ?_
203	  intro V W g
204	  -- Outer `isoMk` naturality square over the thin poset `Opens Y`: the connecting Hom-space is a
205	  -- subsingleton, so the square commutes definitionally (blueprint: thin-poset coherence).
206	  subsingleton

213	noncomputable def presheafDualUnitIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
214	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := Y.presheaf)
215	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
216	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=

226	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
227	      (presheafDualUnitIso (Y := Y)) ≪≫
228	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app

290	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩

296	/-- **The local section of the hom-sheaf manufactured from `f i`** (the load-bearing piece
297	of `homOfLocalCompat`, blueprint `localSection`).  Working with the underlying `Ab`-presheaves
298	`F = M.val.presheaf`, `G = N.val.presheaf`, the presheaf of types
299	`presheafHom F G` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) sends an open `W` to the morphisms of
300	the restrictions of `F`, `G` to the slice `Over W`.  Its value at `U i` is built from the
301	components of `f i`, conjugated by `eqToHom` along the down-set identity
302	`(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` (valid for `V ≤ U i`).  The naturality field — the genuine
303	coherence risk — is automatic on the thin poset `Opens X` once the `eqToHom`-conjugation is
304	peeled, via `Subsingleton.elim` on the hom-sets. -/
305	noncomputable def homLocalSection {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} {ι : Type*}
306	    (U : ι → X.Opens) (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι) (i : ι) :
307	    (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf).obj (op (U i)) where
308	  app W :=
309	    haveI hle : W.unop.left ≤ U i := W.unop.hom.le
310	    haveI himg : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left) = W.unop.left := by
311	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
312	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr hle
313	    M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg.symm)) ≫
314	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W.unop.left)) ≫
315	      N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himg))
316	  naturality := by
317	    intro A B φ
318	    have hBA : (unop B).left ≤ (unop A).left := ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).le
319	    let κ : (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left ⟶ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left :=
320	      (Opens.map (U i).ι.base).map (homOfLE hBA)
321	    have himgA : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop A).left) = (unop A).left := by
322	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
323	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop A).hom.le
324	    have himgB : (U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ (unop B).left) = (unop B).left := by
325	      simp only [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
326	      exact inf_eq_right.mpr (unop B).hom.le
327	    -- naturality of the underlying ab-presheaf morphism of `f i`
328	    have hm := ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).naturality κ.op
329	    -- the two thin-poset square edges agree (`Opens X` is a thin poset)
330	    have hsubM : ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm)
331	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm) ≫ ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op :=
332	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
333	    have hsubN : ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫ eqToHom (congrArg op himgB)
334	        = eqToHom (congrArg op himgA) ≫ ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op :=
335	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
336	    -- M-side: the φ-restriction followed by the `eqToHom` is the `eqToHom` followed by `κ`
337	    have hML : M.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op ≫
338	          M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB.symm))
339	        = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA.symm)) ≫
340	          (M.restrict (U i).ι).val.presheaf.map κ.op := by
341	      rw [(M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubM]
342	      exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
343	    -- N-side analogue
344	    have hNR : N.val.presheaf.map ((U i).ι.opensFunctor.map κ).op ≫
345	          N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgB))
346	        = N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op himgA)) ≫
347	          N.val.presheaf.map ((Over.forget (U i)).map φ.unop).op := by
348	      rw [(N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm, hsubN]
349	      exact N.val.presheaf.map_comp _ _
350	    dsimp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.op_map, Functor.op_obj, Functor.comp_obj]
351	    rw [← Category.assoc, hML]
352	    erw [Category.assoc, reassoc_of% hm, hNR]
353	    simp only [Category.assoc]
354	    rfl

395	Blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` (~L5592).  Let `X` be a scheme,
396	`M N : X.Modules`, and `{U i}` an indexed open cover of `X`.  If for each `i` we have a
