398	that the underlying section maps of `f i` and `f j` agree, *sectionwise*, on every open
399	`V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` (each conjugated into the fixed abelian-group hom-type `M(V) ⟶ N(V)` by the
400	canonical `eqToHom`s from the down-set identity `ι(ι⁻¹V) = V`), then there is a unique global
401	morphism `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules` whose restriction to each `U i` is `f i`.
402	
403	The compatibility hypothesis `hf` is the **sectionwise** overlap-agreement (iter-254 re-sign;
404	this `def` is NOT in `archon-protected.yaml` and has no compiling caller, so the prover owns its
405	signature).  The earlier `HEq` form — comparing the two `Scheme.Modules.pullback`-images of
406	`f i`, `f j` along the two slice-restrictions — was *unsatisfiable*: those images live in
407	sheafifications of pullback presheaves along *different* morphisms, hence in only-isomorphic
408	(not propositionally equal) objects, so no `HEq`-elimination applies and no caller can produce
409	the datum.  The sectionwise form compares only the section maps, which live in the fixed group
410	`M(V) ⟶ N(V)`, and is exactly the data a caller (two local morphisms literally agreeing on
411	overlaps) has at hand.  See blueprint `lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat` sub-step (a).

422	   Step (i) — Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism:
423	   Forget M, N to their underlying sheaves of abelian groups.  The presheaf
424	   `H(W) = Hom_{Ab-preshvs}(M.val.presheaf|_W, N.val.presheaf|_W)` is a sheaf of TYPES:
425	   this is `Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` (Mathlib), consuming the sheaf condition of N.
426	   Convert each `f i` to a local section `s i ∈ H(U i)` via the open-immersion slice
427	   transport `overSliceSheafEquiv` (Vestigial.lean):
428	     - `s i` at a pair `(V, h : V ≤ U i)` is `(f i).val.app` at the corresponding open of
429	       `(U i : Scheme)`, conjugated by `eqToHom` identifications from the down-set identity
430	       `ι_i(ι_i⁻¹(V)) = V` for `V ≤ U i`.  The naturality of `s i` in V is the
431	       section-direction slice of `overSliceSheafEquiv` and is automatic on the thin poset
432	       `Opens X` by `Subsingleton.elim`.
433	   Apply `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing` (or `Presheaf.IsSheaf.existsUnique_gluing`) to
434	   amalgamate the compatible family `(s i)_i` into a unique global section
435	   `s ∈ H(⊤) = (M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)`.
436	   Convert the amalgamated `s` to an ab-presheaf morphism `g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf`
437	   via `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`.
438	
439	   Step (ii) — Promote to `𝒪_X`-linear via `homMk`:
440	   The linearity `g(r • m) = r • g(m)` holds on each `U i` (since `g|_{U i}` comes from
441	   the module morphism `f i`), and the two sides agree globally because the ambient presheaf
442	   is separated.  Apply `Scheme.Modules.homMk g (sectionwise-linearity proof)` to produce
443	   `M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`.
444	
445	   Key sub-lemma to build first (most fragile piece):
446	   The naturality field of `s i` — that the `eqToHom`-conjugated components of `f i` commute
447	   across morphisms of the slice `Over (U i)` — is dominated by `overSliceSheafEquiv` and
448	   should be extracted as a standalone axiom-clean lemma before the full gluing assembly.
449	
450	   Named CLOSED base lemmas:
451	   - `Scheme.Modules.homMk` (TensorObjSubstrate.lean ~L598).
452	   - `Vestigial.overSliceSheafEquiv` (TensorObjSubstrate/Vestigial.lean ~L715).
453	   - `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` (Mathlib) — hom into a sheaf is a sheaf.
454	   - `TopCat.Sheaf.existsUnique_gluing` (Mathlib) — gluing of compatible sections.
455	   - `presheafHomSectionsEquiv` / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` (Mathlib) — top-section ↔ morphism.
456	
457	   Implementation note: this is a MULTI-PIECE BUILD dominated by the `s i` naturality field.
458	   Build `s i` (and its naturality) as a standalone verified lemma FIRST, before assembling
459	   the full gluing.  The step does NOT invoke any tensor stalk — it is purely about gluing
460	   morphisms of sheaves.
461	-/
462	-/
463	noncomputable def homOfLocalCompat {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
464	    {ι : Type*} (U : ι → X.Opens) (hU : ∀ x : X, ∃ i, x ∈ U i)
465	    (f : ∀ i, M.restrict (U i).ι ⟶ N.restrict (U i).ι)
466	    (hf : ∀ (i j : ι) (V : X.Opens) (hVi : V ≤ U i) (hVj : V ≤ U j),
467	        M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U i) hVi).symm)) ≫
468	          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f i).val).app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫
469	            N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U i) hVi)))
470	          = M.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U j) hVj).symm)) ≫
471	              ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (f j).val).app (op ((U j).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫
472	                N.val.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg op (image_preimage_of_le (U j) hVj)))) :
473	    M ⟶ N := by
474	  -- Step (i): glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.  The morphisms-presheaf
475	  -- `presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom`) is a
476	  -- sheaf of types because `N` is a sheaf (`Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom`, consuming `N.isSheaf`).
477	  let H : TopCat.Sheaf (Type u) (X : TopCat) :=
478	    ⟨CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf,
479	      Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf N.isSheaf⟩
480	  -- The cover `{U i}` exhausts `X`, so `iSup U = ⊤`.
481	  have hsup : iSup U = ⊤ := by
482	    rw [eq_top_iff]
483	    intro x _
484	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hU x
485	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨i, hi⟩
486	  -- The compatible family `homLocalSection U f` (its naturality is the load-bearing field,
487	  -- proved axiom-clean above) glues via `existsUnique_gluing` to a unique global section of `H`
488	  -- over `iSup U = ⊤`.  `hglue` records the unique-gluing engine fed with these local sections;
489	  -- it still requires the `IsCompatible` datum, which is exactly the assumed overlap agreement
490	  -- `hf` (transported through `Vestigial.overSliceSheafEquiv`).
491	  have hglue := H.existsUnique_gluing U (fun i => homLocalSection U f i)
492	  -- (a) The cocycle / `IsCompatible` condition: the two restrictions of `homLocalSection i`
493	  -- and `homLocalSection j` to the overlap `U i ⊓ U j` agree as sections of `H`.
494	  have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible
495	      (CategoryTheory.presheafHom M.val.presheaf N.val.presheaf) U
496	      (fun i => homLocalSection U f i) := by
497	    intro i j
498	    refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun Z => ?_)
499	    obtain ⟨W⟩ := Z
500	    erw [presheafHom_map_app W.hom (TopologicalSpace.Opens.infLELeft (U i) (U j)) _ rfl,
501	        presheafHom_map_app W.hom (TopologicalSpace.Opens.infLERight (U i) (U j)) _ rfl]
502	    -- Unfold `homLocalSection` so the goal becomes the explicit sectionwise core equation:
503	    -- at the overlap open `V := W.left ≤ U i ⊓ U j`,
504	    --   LHS = `M.map (eqToHom ..) ≫ (f i).val.app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫ N.map (eqToHom ..)`
505	    --   RHS = `M.map (eqToHom ..) ≫ (f j).val.app (op ((U j).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V)) ≫ N.map (eqToHom ..)`,
506	    -- both in the FIXED `Ab` hom-type `M.val(V) ⟶ N.val(V)`.  With the sectionwise `hf` this is
507	    -- exactly `hf i j W.left _ _` (the `eqToHom`-conjugations match by definitional proof
508	    -- irrelevance; `(Over.mk (W.hom ≫ infLE_))​.left ≡ W.left` defeq).
509	    simp only [homLocalSection]
510	    exact hf i j W.left (W.hom.le.trans inf_le_left) (W.hom.le.trans inf_le_right)
511	  -- (b) Glue and convert the amalgamated `op ⊤`-section to an ab-presheaf morphism `g`.
512	  -- `∃!` is a `Prop`, so the glued section is extracted as a term via `.choose`; `hsup`
513	  -- transports it from `op (iSup U)` to the terminal `op ⊤` that `topSectionToHom` consumes.
514	  refine homMk (topSectionToHom (hsup ▸ (hglue hcompat).choose)) ?_
515	  -- (c) sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linearity of `g = topSectionToHom (glued section)`.  On each `U i`
516	  -- the glued section restricts to `homLocalSection U f i` (the `IsGluing` datum `_hs`), whose
517	  -- components come from the module morphism `f i`, so `g` is `𝒪_X`-linear on opens `≤ U i`;
518	  -- since `{U i}` covers `X` and `N.val.presheaf` is separated (`section_ext`), linearity
519	  -- propagates to every section.  CLOSED (iter-256), axiom-clean: the `section_ext` separatedness
520	  -- reduction, the naturality + `map_smul` reduction to local linearity, the `hconn` connection
521	  -- lemma identifying `g|_{U i}` with `homLocalSection i`, and the inner ring-bridge (native↔
522	  -- `restrictScalars 𝟙` smul bridge `hbridge`, from `Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso` +
523	  -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def'`) feeding the native f-leg linearity `hfl_native` are all
524	  -- in place below — no `sorry` remains in this declaration.
525	  have _hs := (hglue hcompat).choose_spec.1
526	  intro V r m
527	  -- Abbreviate the glued ab-presheaf morphism `g`.
528	  set g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf :=
529	    topSectionToHom (hsup ▸ (hglue hcompat).choose) with hg
530	  -- **Connection lemma.**  On every open `W' ≤ U i`, the glued morphism `g` agrees with the
531	  -- local section `homLocalSection U f i` manufactured from `f i` — this is the content of the
532	  -- `IsGluing` datum `_hs`, transported through the `iSup U = ⊤` identification and the
533	  -- `presheafHom`-restriction calculus.
534	  have hconn : ∀ (i : ι) (W' : X.Opens) (hWi : W' ≤ U i),
535	      g.app (op W') = (homLocalSection U f i).app (op (Over.mk (homOfLE hWi))) := by
536	    intro i W' hWi
537	    have htr : ∀ {a : X.Opens} (h : a = ⊤) (y : H.obj.obj (op a)),
538	        (h ▸ y : H.obj.obj (op ⊤)) = H.obj.map (eqToHom (congrArg op h)) y := by
539	      intro a h y; subst h; simp
540	    rw [hg, topSectionToHom_app, htr hsup]
541	    have hop : eqToHom (congrArg op hsup) = (eqToHom hsup.symm).op := Subsingleton.elim _ _
542	    have hgl : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing H.obj U (fun i => homLocalSection U f i)
543	        (hglue hcompat).choose := _hs
544	    have hsi : (ConcreteCategory.hom (H.obj.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)) (hglue hcompat).choose
545	        = homLocalSection U f i := hgl i
546	    rw [hop, presheafHom_map_app (homOfLE le_top) (eqToHom hsup.symm)
547	        (homOfLE le_top ≫ eqToHom hsup.symm) rfl, ← hsi,
548	      presheafHom_map_app (homOfLE hWi) (Opens.leSupr U i)
549	        (homOfLE hWi ≫ Opens.leSupr U i) rfl]
550	    rw [show (homOfLE le_top ≫ eqToHom hsup.symm : W' ⟶ iSup U)
551	        = (homOfLE hWi ≫ Opens.leSupr U i) from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
552	  -- It suffices, by separatedness of the sheaf `N`, to check the linearity equation on a
553	  -- neighbourhood of each point; we use the cover member `U i` through the point.
554	  refine N.isSheaf.section_ext ?_
555	  intro x hx
556	  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hU x
557	  refine ⟨V.unop ⊓ U i, inf_le_left, ⟨hx, hi⟩, ?_⟩
558	  -- Reduce both sides via naturality of `g` (so the outer `N`-restriction is absorbed into
559	  -- `g.app (op W)`) and the semilinearity of the `M`, `N` restriction maps (`map_smul`) to
560	  -- local linearity of `g` at `W := V.unop ⊓ U i ≤ U i`.
561	  set W : X.Opens := V.unop ⊓ U i with hWdef
562	  have hWV : W ≤ V.unop := inf_le_left
563	  erw [← NatTrans.naturality_apply g (homOfLE hWV).op (r • m),
564	      PresheafOfModules.map_smul M.val (homOfLE hWV).op r m,
565	      PresheafOfModules.map_smul N.val (homOfLE hWV).op r ((g.app V).hom m),
566	      ← NatTrans.naturality_apply g (homOfLE hWV).op m]
567	  -- `g` agrees on `W ≤ U i` with the local section manufactured from the module morphism `f i`;
568	  -- it remains to prove the `homLocalSection`-component is `X.ringCatSheaf(W)`-linear.
569	  rw [hconn i W inf_le_right]
570	  -- The component is the triple composite `M.map (eqToHom e₁) ≫ (f i).val.app P ≫ N.map (eqToHom e₂)`
571	  -- (`P = (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W`).  Decompose it into the three legs.
572	  simp only [homLocalSection]
573	  -- The `homLocalSection`-component at `W` is the triple composite
574	  --   `Φ = M.val.map (eqToHom e₁) ≫ (f i).val.app P ≫ N.val.map (eqToHom e₂)`  (`P = (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W`),
575	  -- an `Ab`-morphism `M(W) ⟶ N(W)`.  We must show `Φ (r' • m') = r' • Φ m'` for the structure
576	  -- scalar `r' = X.ringCatSheaf.map (homOfLE hWV).op r : X.ringCatSheaf(W)`.  Expose the three legs.
577	  erw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
578	       ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
579	       PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_app_apply,
580	       PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
581	  -- Push the scalar through the three legs.  We use the *Γ-level* `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`
582	  -- (which keeps the native `Γ(M, ·)`-module structure) rather than `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
583	  -- (whose semilinear codomain introduces a `restrictScalars`-along-`eqToHom` module that does not
584	  -- match the `f`-leg's `restrictScalars 𝟙` action — `(U i).ι.appIso = Iso.refl`).
585	  -- (a) `M`-leg semilinearity (CLOSED): `M.map e₁ (r' • m') = (X.ring.map e₁ r') • M.map e₁ m'`,
586	  -- with the native `Γ(M, image)`-action on the right (no `restrictScalars` artifact).
587	  erw [Scheme.Modules.map_smul M]
588	  -- (b) `f`-leg `(U i)`-linearity is available as the term `hfl`: `(f i).val.app P` is
589	  -- `(U i).ringCatSheaf(P)`-linear.  Since `(U i).ι.appIso = Iso.refl`
590	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso`), `(U i).ringCatSheaf(P) = Γ(X, image)` and the
591	  -- `(U i)`-action on `M.restrict (U i).ι` is `ModuleCat.restrictScalars 𝟙` of the native action.
592	  have hfl := ((f i).val.app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ
593	    (Over.mk (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ≤ U i))).left))).hom.map_smul
594	  -- **Native↔`restrictScalars 𝟙` smul bridge** for any `K : X.Modules`.  The `(U i)`-action
595	  -- on `K.restrict (U i).ι` is `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` of the native `Γ(X, image)`-action
596	  -- along the structure-ring map `(forget₂ …).map ((U i).ι.appIso _).inv`, which is the identity
597	  -- because `(U i).ι.appIso = Iso.refl` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso`).
598	  have hbridge : ∀ (K : X.Modules) (c : Γ(X, (U i).ι ''ᵁ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W))
599	      (y : Γ(K, (U i).ι ''ᵁ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W)),
600	      (c • y : Γ(K, (U i).ι ''ᵁ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W))
601	        = (c • (show ↑((K.restrict (U i).ι).val.obj (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W))) from y)) := by
602	    intro K c y
603	    erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars.smul_def']
604	    simp [AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso]
605	    rfl
606	  -- **Native `Γ(X, image)`-linearity of the `f`-leg**, bridged from `hfl` via `hbridge`.
607	  have hfl_native : ∀ (c : Γ(X, (U i).ι ''ᵁ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W))
608	      (y : Γ(M, (U i).ι ''ᵁ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W)),
609	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((f i).val.app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W)))) (c • y)
610	        = c • (ConcreteCategory.hom ((f i).val.app (op ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W)))) y := by
611	    intro c y
612	    rw [hbridge M c y]
613	    erw [hfl]
614	    rfl
615	  -- (c) `N`-leg semilinearity (native), pulling the structure scalar back out.
616	  erw [hfl_native, Scheme.Modules.map_smul N]
617	  -- (d) reconcile the `eqToHom`-transported scalars: the two down-set comparison maps `e₁, e₂`
618	  -- compose (through the identity `(U i).ι.appIso`) to `𝟙` on `Γ(X, image)`, since
619	  -- `(U i).ι ''ᵁ ((U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W) = W`; on the thin poset `Opens X` this is `Subsingleton.elim`.
620	  congr 1
621	  simp only [homOfLE_leOfHom, Over.forget_obj, Over.mk_left, Functor.op_obj, sheafCompose_obj_obj,
622	    Functor.comp_obj, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, ObjectProperty.ι_obj, op_unop,
623	    Opens.ι_appIso, Iso.refl_inv, Functor.whiskerRight_app, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_map_hom,
624	    RingHom.toMonoidHom_eq_coe, OneHom.toFun_eq_coe, MonoidHom.toOneHom_coe, MonoidHom.coe_coe,
625	    Functor.comp_map, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
626	  rw [← X.presheaf.map_id (op ((U i).ι ''ᵁ (U i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ W))]
627	  erw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
628	    ← Functor.map_comp]
629	  refine (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (congrArg X.presheaf.map
630	    (Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 (op W)))) _).trans ?_
631	  rw [X.presheaf.map_id]
632	  rfl

9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22	
23	## Status (iter-174 Lane E file-skeleton)
24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after
30	`Picard_RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) lands the étale-sheafification overlay.
31	
32	The 5 pinned declarations are:
33	
34	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.OnProduct` (def, ~5 LOC) — the type of
35	   line bundles on the product `C ×_S T`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection`
37	   (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the pullback map `Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullback_pullback_eq` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
39	   — composition of pullbacks `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*` (Stacks 01HG).
40	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` (def, ~8 LOC)
41	   — well-definedness of the quotient `Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, encoded as
42	   the substantive `Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT` that defines the equivalence
43	   relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ (L')⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`.
44	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.functorial`
45	   (noncomputable def, ~10 LOC) — for `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the induced map
46	   `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')` factoring `g_C^*` through the quotient.

48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
65

88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). iter-187 supplies the missing
96	predicate as a project-side definition via local trivialisation on an affine
97	open cover (see `IsLocallyTrivial` below), and refines `OnProduct` to the
98	corresponding subtype.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- A sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules `M` is **locally trivial of rank one** if every
104	point `x : X` has an affine open neighbourhood `U` on which the restriction
105	`M|_U` is isomorphic to the structure sheaf-as-module `𝒪_U`. This is the
106	project-side stand-in for the missing Mathlib `IsInvertible` predicate on
107	`Scheme.Modules`: line bundles (invertible sheaves) are exactly the
108	`IsLocallyTrivial` modules, by Stacks tag 01HK / Hartshorne II §6. Local
109	trivialisability is the structural content that survives base change along
110	`f : Y ⟶ X` (Stacks 01HH) and is the source of the invertibility-preservation
111	step for `pullbackAlongProjection`.
112	
113	The witness is bundled as `Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit _)`:
114	the existence of a sheaf-of-modules isomorphism over the affine chart. -/
115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
116	  ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
117	    Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)
118	
119	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
120	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
121	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
122	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
123	Picard functor at `T`.
124	
125	iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
126	by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
127	The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
128	matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
129	all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
131	  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }
132	
133	/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
134	sheaf of modules. -/
135	abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
136	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1
137	
138	/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2
141	
142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194	
195	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
196	
197	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
198	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
199	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
200	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
201	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
202	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
203	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
204	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
205	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
208	01HH). -/
209	
210	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
211	curve. Sends a *line bundle on `T`* — i.e. an `O_T`-module `N` equipped with a
212	proof `hN : IsLocallyTrivial N` — to the line bundle `π_T^* N` on `C ×_S T`,
213	induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on `Scheme.Modules` together
214	with the preservation step `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH).
215	
216	iter-187: refined to consume the `OnProduct`-style subtype on the source: the
217	underlying carrier is the direct application of Mathlib's
218	`Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)` functor at `N`, and the
219	locally-trivial witness comes from the project-side helper
220	`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH, named typed sorry pending the affine
221	chart chase). -/
222	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
223	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) (hN : IsLocallyTrivial N) :
224	    OnProduct πC πT :=
225	  ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N,
226	    hN.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)⟩
227	
228	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
229	
230	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
231	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
232	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
233	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
234	
235	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
236	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
237	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
238	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
239	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
240	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
241	
242	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
243	
244	end LineBundle
245	
246	namespace RelPicPresheaf
247	
248	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
249	
250	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
251	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
252	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
253	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
254	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
255	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
256	Picard presheaf at `T`.
257	
258	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
259	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
260	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
261	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
262	
263	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
264	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
265	
266	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
267	
268	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
269	presheaf
270	```
271	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
272	```
273	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
274	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
275	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
276	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
277	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
278	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
279	
280	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
281	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
282	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
283	
284	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
285	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
286	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
287	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
288	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
289	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
290	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
291	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
292	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
293	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
294	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
295	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
296	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
297	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
298	  iseqv :=
299	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
300	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
301	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
302	
303	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
304	
305	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
306	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
307	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
308	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
309	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
310	on quotients
311	```
312	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
313	```
314	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
315	
316	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
317	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
318	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
319	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
320	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
321	
322	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
323	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
324	
325	end RelPicPresheaf
326	
327	end Scheme
328	
329	end AlgebraicGeometry

125	
126	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
127	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:

130	  underlying carrier `LineBundle.OnProduct` was concretised in A.1.b

132	  `{ M : (pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }` (no longer a

134	  product on invertible sheaves modulo the subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`, and
135	  the instance body is real and sorry-free except for the upstream
136	  inverse bridge consumed by `neg` (see Status above).

138	- `PicSharp` is *intended* to return a contravariant group-valued
139	  *functor* whose object and morphism actions are independently

225	/-! ## §1. Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient
226	
227	The Picard group `Pic(X)` of any scheme is canonically an abelian group
228	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
229	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
230	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of
231	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection` (A.1.b)
232	respects this structure: it sends `O_T ↦ O_{C ×_k T}` (the structure
233	sheaf is preserved by inverse image) and is multiplicative on tensor
234	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
235	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).
236	
237	Therefore `π_T^* Pic(T)` is a subgroup of `Pic(C ×_k T)`, and the
238	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
239	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective
240	homomorphism with kernel exactly `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
241	
242	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Kleiman §2,
243	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01CR). -/
244	
245	/-! ### Substrate for the relative-Picard group law (iter-247)
246	
247	**Architectural note (iter-247 — import cycle RESOLVED).** Iter-246 was forced to
248	reproduce the tensor substrate as local pure-Mathlib copies because
249	`TensorObjSubstrate.lean` then *imported* `RelPicFunctor.lean`, putting the substrate
250	downstream of this file. The iter-247 refactor broke that cycle — dependency now
251	flows `LineBundlePullback → TensorObjSubstrate → RelPicFunctor` — so this file
252	imports `TensorObjSubstrate` and cites the real upstream substrate directly. All the
253	local copies are deleted (see the rewire note above).

255	The carrier setoid `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` is the **iso-class** equivalence
256	`Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)` on the locally-trivial line bundles on `C ×_S T`
257	(`LineBundlePullback.lean:349`), *not* the quotient-by-`H_T` relation. Hence
258	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is `Pic(C ×_S T)` itself, and the honest
259	`AddCommGroup` on it is the **tensor-product Picard group** — the additive mirror of
260	the absolute `picCommGroup` (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:813`). The blueprint's Step 2–4
261	(`pullbackHom`, `H_T := pullbackHom.range`, setoid reconciliation, transport) describe
262	a *different* future carrier (once `preimage_subgroup` is refined to quotient by
263	`H_T`); they do not apply to the present iso-class carrier.

265	The group law is built directly here from upstream citations. The coherence data
266	(`Modules.tensorObj`, `Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso`, the unitors, the braiding,
267	`Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`,
268	`Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`) are all sorry-free and axiom-clean upstream.
269	The group axioms `add_comm`/`zero_add`/`add_zero`/`add_assoc` are fully `sorry`-free,
270	and `zero` uses the proven `isLocallyTrivial_unit`. The **single** residual obligation
271	is the additive inverse `neg`/`neg_add_cancel`, which consumes

273	`IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible` (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:672`), a genuine

277	/-! ### iter-247 rewire: cite the upstream substrate directly.
278	
279	The iter-246 local pure-Mathlib copies (`pTensor`, `pTensorIso`, `pLeftUnitor`,
280	`pRightUnitor`, `pBraiding`) and the four typed-`sorry` bridges
281	(`pTensor_isLocallyTrivial`, `pAssoc`, `exists_pTensor_inverse`,

296	/-- The structure sheaf is locally trivial (it restricts to the structure sheaf on
297	every affine open). iter-247: closed by routing through the proven upstream
298	`Modules.pullbackUnitIso` (the iter-246 `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)`
299	instance-resolution quirk is side-stepped — `pullbackUnitIso` already bundles the
300	`Final`/iso machinery). On any affine chart `W ∋ x`: the restriction of the unit is
301	its pullback (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`), and the pullback of the unit is the
302	unit (`pullbackUnitIso`); compose to trivialise `(𝒪_X)|_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. -/
303	private theorem isLocallyTrivial_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
304	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
305	  -- iter-247: closed by routing through the proven upstream `pullbackUnitIso`
306	  -- (the iter-246 `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ)` instance-resolution quirk is
307	  -- side-stepped — `pullbackUnitIso` already bundles the `Final`/iso machinery).
308	  -- On any affine chart `W ∋ x`: restriction of the unit is its pullback
309	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`), and the pullback of the unit is the unit
310	  -- (`pullbackUnitIso`); compose to trivialise `(𝒪_X)|_W ≅ 𝒪_W`.
311	  intro x
312	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, _⟩ :=
313	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := ⊤)
314	      (show x ∈ (⊤ : X.Opens) from trivial)
315	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ?_⟩
316	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app _ ≪≫ Modules.pullbackUnitIso W.ι⟩
317	
318	/-- Uniqueness of the tensor inverse up to iso (mirror of `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`).
319	iter-247: rewired to cite the upstream coherence isos directly. -/
320	private theorem pInverseUnique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
321	    (e : Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
322	    (e' : Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
323	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
324	  ⟨(Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
325	    Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
326	    (Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := N) (N := M) (P := N')).symm ≪≫
327	    Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Modules.tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
328	    Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
329	
330	/-- The addition carrier: `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, lifted to the loc-triv carrier
331	`OnProduct`. iter-247: this is exactly the upstream `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`
332	(moved into this file by the iter-247 refactor). -/
333	private noncomputable def relTensorObj {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
334	    (L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT :=
335	  Modules.tensorObjOnProduct πC πT L L'
336	
337	/-- Descended addition on the relative Picard quotient: well-defined on iso-classes
338	by bifunctoriality (`Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso`). Mirror of `picMul`. -/
339	private noncomputable def relAdd {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S} :
340	    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →
341	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →
342	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) :=
343	  Quotient.lift₂
344	    (fun L L' => Quotient.mk _ (relTensorObj L L'))
345	    (by
346	      rintro L L' M M' ⟨e⟩ ⟨e'⟩
347	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso e e'⟩)
348	
349	/-- Descended negation on the relative Picard quotient: `-[L] := [Linv]` for the
350	inverse witness of `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`, well-defined by
351	`pInverseUnique`. Mirror of `picInv`. -/
352	private noncomputable def relNeg {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S} :
353	    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →
354	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) :=
355	  Quotient.lift
356	    (fun L => Quotient.mk _
357	      (⟨Classical.choose (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial),
358	        (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial)).1⟩ :
359	        LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT))
360	    (by
361	      rintro L M ⟨e⟩
362	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
363	      have h1 :=
364	        (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some
365	      have h2 := Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso e (Iso.refl _) ≪≫
366	        (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse M.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some
367	      exact pInverseUnique h1 h2)
368	
369	/-- **Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient.**
370	
371	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test
372	object `πT : T ⟶ S`, the quotient set
373	```
374	Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)
375	  = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
376	```
377	carries a canonical abelian-group structure: addition is the descent of
378	tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, the zero element is the class
379	`[O_{C ×_S T}]`, and the inverse is `-[L] := [L⁻¹]` (the dual line
380	bundle). Well-definedness on the quotient uses the subgroup property of
381	`π_T^* Pic(T)` from `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid`.
382	
383	iter-247 state: this instance now has a **real, sorry-free body**.
384	A.1.b's `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier was concretised in
385	`LineBundlePullback.lean` (iter-188) as
386	`{ M : (pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }`, and the
387	tensor-product group law is built directly from the upstream substrate
388	`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`
389	(`Modules.tensorObj`, `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`, the coherence isos
390	`Modules.tensorObj_{assoc_iso,left_unitor,right_unitor,braiding}`) —
391	not from a `Scheme.Modules` monoidal-category instance. The only
392	`sorry` reached is *upstream*: `neg`/`neg_add_cancel` consume
393	`Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse` (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:670`), the
394	deferred reverse bridge `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible`. There is no
395	file-local `addCommGroup` sorry and no Mathlib monoidal-upgrade gate
396	(cf. `LineBundlePullback.lean` L344--L346 for the historical note). -/
397	-- iter-247 Lane RPF: the real tensor-product Picard group on the iso-class
398	-- quotient (additive mirror of `picCommGroup`), now built from DIRECT citations of
399	-- the upstream substrate (`Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, the unitors, the braiding,
400	-- `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`, `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`); the iter-246
401	-- local pure-Mathlib copies were deleted once the import cycle was broken. The
402	-- operation `relAdd` (well-defined via `Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso`) and the axioms
403	-- `add_comm`/`zero_add`/`add_zero`/`add_assoc` are fully `sorry`-free; `zero` uses
404	-- the proven `isLocallyTrivial_unit`. The ONLY residual obligation is
405	-- `neg`/`neg_add_cancel`, which consume `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse` — the
406	-- single genuine upstream project-deferred sorry (the reverse bridge
407	-- `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible`, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:672`).
408	-- `nsmul`/`zsmul` carry no field default in `AddMonoid`/`SubNegMonoid`
409	-- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.lean:641`), and the canonical `nsmulRec`/`zsmulRec`
410	-- need `Zero`/`Add`/`Neg` instances that are not yet in scope mid-structure; we
411	-- supply them via `letI` so `nsmulRec`/`zsmulRec` elaborate (standard idiom).
412	noncomputable instance addCommGroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
413	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
414	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :=
415	  letI iZero : Zero (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :=
416	    ⟨Quotient.mk _ (⟨SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf,
417	      isLocallyTrivial_unit⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)⟩
418	  letI iAdd : Add (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) := ⟨relAdd⟩
419	  letI iNeg : Neg (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) := ⟨relNeg⟩
420	  { add := relAdd
421	    zero := Quotient.mk _
422	      (⟨SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf,
423	        isLocallyTrivial_unit⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)
424	    neg := relNeg
425	    nsmul := nsmulRec
426	    zsmul := zsmulRec
427	    add_assoc := by
428	      rintro a b c
429	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
430	      induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
431	      induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
432	      exact Quotient.sound
433	        ⟨Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := a.carrier) (N := b.carrier) (P := c.carrier)⟩
434	    zero_add := by
435	      rintro a
436	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
437	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor a.carrier⟩
438	    add_zero := by
439	      rintro a
440	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
441	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor a.carrier⟩
442	    neg_add_cancel := by
443	      rintro a
444	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
445	      exact Quotient.sound
446	        ⟨Modules.tensorObj_braiding _ a.carrier ≪≫
447	          (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse a.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some⟩
448	    add_comm := by
449	      rintro a b
450	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
451	      induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
452	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObj_braiding a.carrier b.carrier⟩ }
453	
454	end PicSharp

456	/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor
457	
458	We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
459	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
460	instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor
461	
462	```
463	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
464	```
465	
466	sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group
467	`Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (with the structure of §1), and a
468	morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
469	`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
470	line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.
471	
472	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
473	
474	/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
475	integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor
476	
477	```
478	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
479	```
480	
481	On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, the abelian-group
482	quotient of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Lean instance
483	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`).
484	
485	On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
486	`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
487	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
488	`PicSharp.functorial` below).
489	
490	Universe: object map values are `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` because the
491	underlying carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` lives in
492	`Type (u+1)` (since `LineBundle.OnProduct` is defined to land in
493	`Type (u+1)`).
494	
495	**Tracked gap (cross-file, Lane TS D4′).** The body is presently a
496	`Functor.const`-style **stub** at
497	`AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit.{u+2} : AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` — *not* the
498	intended group-valued assignment. This is a deliberate stub, not a
499	"placeholder pending a file-local sorry": the §1 `addCommGroup`
500	instance already has a real body (see the module Status), so no
501	file-local sorry gates this def.
502	
503	The intended construction
504	`obj T := AddCommGrpCat.of (Quotient (preimage_subgroup _C.hom T.unop.hom))`
505	with `map := PicSharp.functorial` needs `functorial` to be a genuine
506	group hom on each fibre — i.e. needs `functorial`'s `map_add`, which
507	says line-bundle pullback `(id_C ×_S g)^*` preserves the tensor-product
508	group law. That preservation is the **loc-triv comparison iso**
509	`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv` (Lane TS D4′, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`,
510	blueprint `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`), giving
511	`IsInvertible.pullback`, **not yet landed** (the D2′ η-bridge is the
512	active critical path upstream). Until D4′ lands, the stub keeps the
513	file-skeleton well-typed: downstream consumers (`PicSharp.presheaf`,
514	`PicSharp.etSheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf_group_structure`) only use the
515	*group-valued presheaf signature*, not the object assignment, so the
516	sheafification machinery elaborates against the stub. The signature is
517	pinned and preserved verbatim when the real functor lands. -/
518	noncomputable def PicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
519	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
520	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
521	  (CategoryTheory.Functor.const _).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of (PUnit.{u+2}))
522	
523	namespace PicSharp
524	
525	/-! ## §3. Functoriality (group-homomorphism strengthening)
526	
527	The naturality lemma `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of A.1.b produces, for
528	each morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, a set map
529	```
530	g^♯ : Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T) ⟶ Pic(C ×_S T') / π_{T'}^* Pic(T')
531	```
532	of quotient sets. Combined with the abelian-group instance of §1 on
533	both sides, this set map upgrades to an additive-monoid homomorphism
534	`AddMonoidHom`: indeed `g_C^*` preserves tensor products and the
535	structure sheaf, so it preserves the abelian-group operations on both
536	sides; the upgrade is the substantive content of
537	`lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial`.
538	
539	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial` (Kleiman §2,
540	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
541	
542	/-! ## §4. Wrapping as a functor instance
543	
544	The group-valued presheaf bundling: combine the on-objects assignment
545	of `PicSharp` (carrying the `addCommGroup` structure) with the
546	on-morphisms assignment of `functorial` (each a group hom) into a
547	single functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`. The identity /
548	composition laws of the functor are inherited from the corresponding
549	identities on `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, descended through the
550	quotient.
551	
552	This wrapper is kept distinct from `PicSharp` to mirror the blueprint
553	split (`def:rel_pic_sharp` vs.\ `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`) and to
554	record the explicit re-packaging step from the lemma-by-lemma data
555	(`addCommGroup`, `PicSharp`, `functorial`) into a single
556	category-theoretic functor — useful when applying functorial
557	constructions (Yoneda, sheafification, …) to the *bundled* form.
558	
559	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` (Kleiman §2,
560	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
561	
562	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf, bundled.**
563	
564	The relative Picard functor `PicSharp_{C/k}` packaged as a single
565	contravariant functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, with object
566	action `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (group structure
567	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`) and morphism action `g ↦ g^♯` (group hom from
568	`PicSharp.functorial`). Identity and composition laws are inherited
569	from `Scheme.Modules.pullbackId` / `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`
570	descended through the quotient.
571	
572	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: the body re-exports `PicSharp _C` (the
573	on-objects/on-morphisms data of §2 already produces a functor into
574	`AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`). The chapter splits the "object/morphism
575	description" (`PicSharp`) and the "bundled functor" (`presheaf`) to
576	mirror the blueprint partition `def:rel_pic_sharp` /
577	`thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`; on the Lean side they are the same data,
578	so `presheaf := PicSharp _C` is a clean closure.
579	
580	Note on naming: this is the canonical "fully assembled" form mentioned
581	as `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` in the blueprint; it is structurally a
582	`def` (not a `theorem`) because the conclusion is a `Functor` (data),
583	not a `Prop`. -/
584	noncomputable def presheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
585	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
586	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
587	  PicSharp _C
588	
589	end PicSharp

591	/-! ## §5. Étale sheafification
592	
593	The relative Picard presheaf `PicSharp_{C/k}` is generally not even a
594	Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2 L1292–L1302). To obtain a representable
595	functor in the sense of `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`
596	(downstream A.2.c), one replaces `PicSharp_{C/k}` by its **associated
597	étale sheaf** `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{∼ét}`. Kleiman §4
598	(Theorem `th:main`) represents precisely this sheafified functor.
599	
600	For the iter-176 file-skeleton, we encode the sheafification as
601	`presheafToSheaf J _` applied to the bundled `PicSharp.presheaf` for a
602	**parameter** Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` — because
603	Mathlib at the pinned commit does not ship an étale Grothendieck
604	topology on schemes (it ships only the morphism property
605	`AlgebraicGeometry.Etale`). iter-177+ refinement: bind `J` to the
606	canonical étale topology once it lands in Mathlib.
607	
608	*Naming note*: the blueprint pins this declaration under
609	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`, which already names the
610	*representing scheme* in `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`. We use
611	the namespaced name `PicSharp.etSheaf` here; the blueprint will be
612	updated by the plan/review agents.
613	
614	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification` (Kleiman §2,
615	`df:Pfs` étale-sheaf clause). -/
616	
617	namespace PicSharp
618	
619	/-! ## §6. Sheafification preserves the abelian-group structure
620	
621	The étale sheafification of an abelian-group-valued presheaf is itself
622	abelian-group-valued: the forgetful functor `AddCommGrpCat ⥤ Type`
623	preserves filtered colimits (these are computed pointwise), and the
624	plus-construction is built from filtered colimits, so sheafification
625	commutes with the forgetful functor up to canonical isomorphism. The
626	substantive content is the **sheafification unit**: a canonical
627	morphism of presheaves `PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf ?).obj`
628	(in `AddCommGrpCat`) that exhibits the universal property of
629	sheafification.
630	
631	This statement does NOT have an explicit `\lean{...}` pin in the
632	blueprint (`thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` is a description
633	rather than a Lean target); we surface it here so the downstream
634	representability statement (`chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`) can
635	cite the unit directly.
636	
637	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` (Kleiman
638	§2, `df:Pfs`; standard `Sheafification.toSheafify` content). -/

640	end PicSharp

31	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base change along a ring iso commutes
32	with `⊗` (the H2 "bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`)
33	
34	For a *ring isomorphism* `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `A`, `B`, base change along
35	`e` (giving each `S`-module its `R`-module structure via `Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom`)
36	commutes with the tensor product: the canonical map `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b` is an
37	`R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B`. Equivalently, `restrictScalars`
38	along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal — the lax tensorator is invertible. Mathlib
39	has `ModuleCat.extendScalars` strong monoidal but `restrictScalars` only
40	`LaxMonoidal`; this ring-iso strong upgrade is absent and is the documented "REAL
41	bottom gap" (H2) of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
42	
43	section RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
44	
45	open TensorProduct
46	
47	variable {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
48	
49	/-- The `R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B` (`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ b`),
50	where the `R`-module structures are base-changed along the ring iso `e : R ≃+* S`.
51	Base change along a ring iso commutes with `⊗`. -/
52	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
53	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] :
54	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
55	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
56	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
57	    TensorProduct R A B ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B := by
58	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
59	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
60	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
61	  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`, an `R`-bilinear-to-linear lift.
62	  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
63	    TensorProduct.lift
64	      { toFun := fun a =>
65	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
66	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
67	            map_smul' := fun r b => by
68	              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
69	              change a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
70	              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
71	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
72	        map_smul' := fun r a => by
73	          ext b
74	          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
75	            AddHom.coe_mk]
76	          change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
77	          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
78	  -- Inverse: `a ⊗ₜ[S] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[R] b`. Built as an additive lift out of the
79	  -- `S`-tensor (scalar-swap compatibility uses `s • a = e.symm s •ᵣ a`), then
80	  -- shown `R`-linear (`R` acting on the `S`-tensor via `e`).
81	  let bwdAdd : TensorProduct S A B →+ TensorProduct R A B :=
82	    TensorProduct.liftAddHom
83	      { toFun := fun a =>
84	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
85	            map_zero' := by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
86	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add] }
87	        map_zero' := by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]
88	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
89	      (fun s a b => by
90	        simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
91	        -- `(s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b)`; move the `S`-scalar through `e.symm`.
92	        have hsa : (s • a) = (e.symm s : R) • a := by
93	          change s • a = e (e.symm s) • a; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
94	        have hsb : (s • b) = (e.symm s : R) • b := by
95	          change s • b = e (e.symm s) • b; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
96	        rw [hsa, hsb]; exact TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _ )
97	  let bwd : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct R A B :=
98	    { toFun := bwdAdd
99	      map_add' := bwdAdd.map_add
100	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
101	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
102	        -- `R` acts on the `S`-tensor via `e`; reduce to additive `S`-scalar action.
103	        change bwdAdd (e r • x) = r • bwdAdd x
104	        induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
105	        | zero => simp
106	        | tmul a b =>
107	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
108	            change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = r • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
109	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
110	            rfl
111	        | add x y hx hy =>
112	            rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy, smul_add] }
113	  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear fwd bwd ?_ ?_
114	  · -- right inverse `fwd ∘ bwd = id`. The composite is `R`-linear over the
115	    -- `S`-tensor, so check on additive generators by induction.
116	    refine LinearMap.ext fun x => ?_
117	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
118	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
119	    | zero => simp
120	    | tmul a b =>
121	        change fwd (bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
122	        change fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
123	        simp only [fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
124	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add bwd, map_add fwd, hx, hy]
125	  · -- left inverse `bwd ∘ fwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b` (composite `R`-linear over the
126	    -- `R`-tensor, so `TensorProduct.ext'` applies).
127	    refine TensorProduct.ext' fun a b => ?_
128	    change bwdAdd (fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
129	    change bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
130	    rfl
131	
132	/-- The forward map of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` on a simple tensor:
133	`a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`. -/
134	@[simp] lemma restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul (e : R ≃+* S)
135	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] (a : A) (b : B) :
136	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
137	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
138	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
139	    restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e A B (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b := by
140	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
141	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
142	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
143	  simp only [restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv, LinearEquiv.ofLinear_apply,
144	    TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
145	
146	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is strong monoidal: the
147	lax tensorator `μ` is an isomorphism.** This is the "REAL bottom gap" (H2) of
148	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. For a ring iso `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `M₁, M₂`,
149	the underlying map of the lax tensorator
150	`μ : restrictScalars(M₁) ⊗_R restrictScalars(M₂) ⟶ restrictScalars(M₁ ⊗_S M₂)`
151	sends `m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ↦ m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul`), which is exactly
152	the forward `R`-linear equivalence `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e`, hence is
153	bijective, hence an isomorphism. Mathlib has `extendScalars` strong monoidal but
154	only `restrictScalars` lax; this ring-iso strong upgrade is the documented absent
155	ingredient. -/
156	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ (e : R ≃+* S) (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
157	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) := by
158	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
159	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂)
160	      = ⇑(restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂) := by
161	    ext z
162	    induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
163	    | zero => rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm
164	    | tmul a b =>
165	        erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul]
166	        exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul e M₁ M₂ a b).symm
167	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add, hx, hy]; exact (map_add _ x y).symm
168	  rw [hfun]
169	  exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂).bijective
170	
171	/-- **The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is an
172	isomorphism.** Its underlying map is the ring map `e` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`),
173	which is bijective since `e` is a ring equivalence. -/
174	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε (e : R ≃+* S) :
175	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) := by
176	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
177	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) = ⇑e := by
178	    ext r
179	    exact ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η (f := e.toRingHom) r
180	  rw [hfun]
181	  exact e.bijective
182	
183	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is (strong) monoidal.**
184	The packaged `Functor.Monoidal` structure obtained from the lax structure by
185	inverting `ε` (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε`) and `μ` (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ`). This
186	is the clean, reusable strong-monoidal upgrade that Mathlib provides for
187	`extendScalars` but not `restrictScalars`; it is the ModuleCat-level core of the H2
188	ingredient of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
189	@[implicit_reducible]
190	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv (e : R ≃+* S) :
191	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom).Monoidal := by
192	  haveI : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) :=
193	    restrictScalars_isIso_ε e
194	  haveI : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) :=
195	    fun M₁ M₂ => restrictScalars_isIso_μ e M₁ M₂
196	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
197	
198	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal tensorator.** For an arbitrary
199	*bijective* ring hom `f : R →+* S`, the lax tensorator of `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f`
200	is an isomorphism. This is the form consumed by the presheaf-level lift, where the
201	componentwise ring map `(α.app X).hom` of a `NatIso` of ring presheaves is bijective
202	but not literally presented as `(_ : R ≃+* S).toRingHom`. -/
203	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f)
204	    (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
205	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f) M₁ M₂) := by
206	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
207	  rw [hfe]
208	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_μ (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf) M₁ M₂
209	
210	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal unit.** Companion of
211	`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective` for the lax unit `ε`. -/
212	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f) :
213	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f)) := by
214	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
215	  rw [hfe]
216	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_ε (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf)
217	
218	/-- **Base change along a ring iso commutes with the linear dual** (the `Hom`-analogue
219	of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv`). For a ring isomorphism `e : R ≃+* S` and an
220	`S`-module `M`, the `R`-linear equivalence
221	`restrictScalars_e (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (restrictScalars_e M →ₗ[R] R)`, `φ ↦ e.symm ∘ φ`.
222	
223	This is the ModuleCat-level **H2′ ingredient** of the (still-open, d.2-FREE) C-bridge
224	`(dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f)` along an open immersion `f`: the C-bridge
225	mirrors `tensorObj_restrict_iso` exactly (Step 1 `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Step 2
226	`sheafificationCompPullback`, Step 3 strip sheafification, Step 4 H1
227	`pushforwardPushforwardAdj`∘`leftAdjointUniq` ∘ this H2′), with `⊗` replaced by `dual`
228	and `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` replaced by this equivalence. Like its tensor
229	counterpart it is **pure algebra over a ring iso — no stalk, no filtered-colimit, no
230	whiskering of the sheafification unit (no d.2)**: a map `M → S` is `R`-linear (via `e`)
231	iff it is `S`-linear, and `e.symm` carries the codomain `S` to `R`. The forward map is
232	`R`-linear; the inverse `ψ ↦ e ∘ ψ` is its two-sided inverse. The `iMS` `Module R`
233	instance on `M →ₗ[S] S` is base change along `e`. -/
234	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
235	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] :
236	    letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
237	    letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
238	    (M →ₗ[S] S) ≃ₗ[R] (M →ₗ[R] R) := by
239	  letI _iM : Module R M := Module.compHom M e.toRingHom
240	  letI _iMS : Module R (M →ₗ[S] S) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
241	  have hsmulM : ∀ (r : R) (m : M), r • m = e r • m := fun _ _ => rfl
242	  refine
243	    { toFun := fun φ =>
244	        { toFun := fun m => e.symm (φ m)
245	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
246	          map_smul' := fun r m => by
247	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
248	            rw [hsmulM, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
249	      invFun := fun ψ =>
250	        { toFun := fun m => e (ψ m)
251	          map_add' := fun m m' => by simp [map_add]
252	          map_smul' := fun s m => by
253	            simp only [RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul]
254	            have hsm : s • m = (e.symm s) • m := by rw [hsmulM, e.apply_symm_apply]
255	            rw [hsm, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.apply_symm_apply] }
256	      left_inv := fun φ => by ext m; exact e.apply_symm_apply _
257	      right_inv := fun ψ => by ext m; exact e.symm_apply_apply _
258	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => by
259	        ext m
260	        simp only [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk, map_add]
261	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
262	        ext m
263	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
264	          smul_eq_mul]
265	        change e.symm ((e r • φ) m) = r * e.symm (φ m)
266	        rw [LinearMap.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, map_mul, e.symm_apply_apply] }
267	
268	end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
