270	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
271	
272	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
273	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
274	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
275	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
276	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
277	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
278	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
279	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
280	
281	namespace PresheafOfModules
282	
283	universe v'
284	
285	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
286	
287	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
288	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
289	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
290	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
291	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
292	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
293	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
294	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
295	  naturality {X Y} f := by
296	    ext r
297	    dsimp
298	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
299	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
300	    simp only [map_one]
301	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
302	
303	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
304	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
305	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
306	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
307	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
308	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
309	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
310	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
311	  app X := by
312	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
313	  naturality {X Y} f := by
314	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
315	    dsimp
316	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
317	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
318	    rfl
319	
320	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
321	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
322	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
323	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
324	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
325	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
326	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
327	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
328	  μ_natural_left := by
329	    intro X Y f X'
330	    ext1 Z
331	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
332	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
333	  μ_natural_right := by
334	    intro X Y X' f
335	    ext1 Z
336	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
337	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
338	  associativity := by
339	    intro M N P
340	    ext1 Z
341	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
342	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
343	  left_unitality := by
344	    intro M
345	    ext1 Z
346	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
347	      (M.obj Z)
348	  right_unitality := by
349	    intro M
350	    ext1 Z
351	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
352	      (M.obj Z)
353	
354	end PresheafOfModules
355	
356	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)
357	
358	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
359	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
360	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
361	`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates
362	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
363	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
364	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
365	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
366	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
367	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
368	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/
369	
370	namespace PresheafOfModules
371	
372	open CategoryTheory Functor
373	
374	section PushforwardNatTrans
375	
376	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
377	
378	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
379	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
380	  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)
381	
382	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
383	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
384	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
385	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
386	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
387	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
388	  app M :=
389	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
390	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
391	        ext x
392	        dsimp
393	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
394	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
395	        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
396	  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
397	    ext U x
398	    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
399	
400	end PushforwardNatTrans
401	
402	section PushforwardCongr
403	
404	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
405	
406	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
407	  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
408	
409	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of
410	rings are isomorphic. De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`
411	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the
412	presheaf-level `pushforward` lands in `PresheafOfModules` directly). -/
413	noncomputable def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ) :
414	    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
415	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
416	    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
417	      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
418	        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
419	      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)
420	
421	end PushforwardCongr
422	
423	section PushforwardAdj
424	
425	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
426	
427	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
428	  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
429	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
430	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
431	  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)
432	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R = ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ)
433	  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)
434	
435	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
436	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`.** If `F ⊣ G`, then the presheaf-of-modules
437	pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
438	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the
439	sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s and the `.val`/`.hom` wrappers. This is the H1 linchpin: applied
440	to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
441	`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
442	noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
443	  unit :=
444	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
445	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
446	  counit :=
447	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
448	      (pushforwardCongr (by simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
449	  left_triangle_components X := by
450	    ext U x
451	    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
452	      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
453	    dsimp only [id_obj]
454	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
455	    simp
456	  right_triangle_components X := by
457	    ext U x
458	    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
459	      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
460	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
461	    simp
462	
463	end PushforwardAdj
464	
465	section StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
466	
467	universe v'
468	
469	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C]
470	
471	/-- **A sectionwise-isomorphism morphism of presheaves of modules is an isomorphism.**
472	The inverse is assembled sectionwise via `isoMk` (whose forward naturality is exactly the
473	naturality of the given morphism). -/
474	lemma isIso_of_isIso_app {𝓡 : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} 𝓡}
475	    (g : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ U, IsIso (g.app U)) : IsIso g := by
476	  haveI := h
477	  have hg : g = (PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun U => asIso (g.app U))
478	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=
479	    PresheafOfModules.hom_ext (fun _ => rfl)
480	  rw [hg]; infer_instance
481	
482	variable {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
483	
484	/-- **`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α` is strong monoidal when `α` is sectionwise
485	bijective.** The lax tensorator `μ` and unit `ε` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`) are assembled
486	sectionwise from the `ModuleCat`-level ones, which are isomorphisms for a bijective ground-ring
487	map (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective`); hence the
488	presheaf `μ`/`ε` are sectionwise isos, hence isos (`isIso_of_isIso_app`), and the lax structure
489	upgrades to strong via `Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal`. This is the **H2** presheaf lift of
490	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
491	@[implicit_reducible]
492	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective
493	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
494	    (hα : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (α.app U).hom) :
495	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Monoidal := by
496	  haveI hε : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α)) :=
497	    isIso_of_isIso_app _ (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U))
498	  haveI hμ : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) M₁ M₂) :=
499	    fun M₁ M₂ => isIso_of_isIso_app _
500	      (fun U => restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (α.app U).hom (hα U) (M₁.obj U) (M₂.obj U))
501	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
502	
503	end StrongMonoidalRestrictScalars
504	
505	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
506	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
507	
508	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
509	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
510	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
511	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
512	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
513	
514	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
515	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
516	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
517	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
518	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
519	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
520	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
521	
522	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
523	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
524	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
525	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
526	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
527	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
528	project-local supplement. -/
529	
530	namespace InternalHom
531	
532	open CategoryTheory Limits
533	
534	universe vC uC
535	
536	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
537	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
538	
539	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
540	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
541	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
542	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
543	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
544	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
545	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
546	
547	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
548	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
549	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
550	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
551	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
552	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
553	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
554	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
555	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
556	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
557	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
558	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
559	
560	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
561	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
562	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
563	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
564	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
565	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
566	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
567	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
568	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
569	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))
570	    (by
571	      intro X Y g; ext m
572	      change (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y)
573	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) m)
574	          = (ConcreteCategory.hom (N.map g)) ((LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj X : Type u) (N.obj X)
575	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)) m)
576	      rw [LinearMap.lsmul_apply, LinearMap.lsmul_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.map_smul]
577	      rw [termRingMap_naturality]; rfl)
578	
579	variable {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
580	
581	/-- The section-wise action of `globalSMul f`: at `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by
582	`termRingMap Y f`. -/
583	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
584	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
585	
586	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
587	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
588	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
589	
590	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
591	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
592	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
593	
594	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
595	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
596	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
597	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
598	
599	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
600	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
601	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
602	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
603	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
604	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
605	
606	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
607	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
608	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
609	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
610	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
611	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
612	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
613	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
614	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
615	@[implicit_reducible]
616	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
617	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
618	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
619	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
620	  mul_smul f g φ := by
621	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
622	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
623	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
624	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
625	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
626	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
627	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
628	  add_smul f g φ := by
629	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
630	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
631	
632	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
633	
634	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
635	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
636	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
637	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
638	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
639	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
640	applied to that terminal. -/
641	
642	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
643	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
644	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
645	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
646	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
647	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
648	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
649	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
650	
651	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
652	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
653	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
654	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
655	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
656	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
657	@[implicit_reducible]
658	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)
659	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
660	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u) (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :=
661	  homModule (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U)) Over.mkIdTerminal
662	    (restr U M) (restr U N)
663	
664	/-- **The restriction map of the presheaf internal hom.** For a morphism `g : V ⟶ U`
665	of `C`, restricting a morphism `φ : M|_U ⟶ N|_U` of restricted presheaves of modules
666	along `Over.map g : Over V ⥤ Over U` yields a morphism `M|_V ⟶ N|_V`. Realised as
667	`(pushforward₀ (Over.map g) …).map φ`; the target base ring presheaf
668	`(Over.map g).op ⋙ (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R` is definitionally `(Over.forget V).op ⋙ R`,
669	so the result has the expected type `restr V M ⟶ restr V N`. This is the
670	further-restriction map `φ ↦ φ|_V` of blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
671	noncomputable def restrictionMap {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
672	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
673	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) : restr V M ⟶ restr V N :=
674	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.map g)
675	    ((Over.forget U).op ⋙ (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map φ
676	
677	/-- **`restrictionMap` is additive.** Part of the additivity assertion of blueprint
678	`lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`. -/
679	lemma restrictionMap_add {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
680	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
681	    (φ ψ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
682	    restrictionMap g (φ + ψ) = restrictionMap g φ + restrictionMap g ψ := by
683	  ext1 X; rfl
684	
685	/-- **`restrictionMap` preserves zero.** -/
686	lemma restrictionMap_zero {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
687	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} :
688	    restrictionMap g (0 : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) = 0 := by
689	  ext1 X; rfl
690	
691	/-- Helper: the component of a morphism of presheaves of modules at two *equal*
692	objects agrees up to `HEq`. Proven by `subst`. Used to discharge the
693	pseudofunctoriality coherence of `restrictionMap` (`Over.map` is only functorial in
694	its argument up to `Over.mapId_eq` / `Over.mapComp_eq`). -/
695	private lemma hom_app_heq {B : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} B}
696	    (φ : M ⟶ N) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (h : X = Y) : HEq (φ.app X) (φ.app Y) := by
697	  subst h; rfl
698	
699	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: identity.** -/
700	lemma restrictionMap_id {U : C}
701	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
702	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
703	    restrictionMap (𝟙 U) φ = φ := by
704	  ext1 X
705	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapId_eq]; rfl))
706	
707	/-- **Functoriality of `restrictionMap`: composition.** For `g : V ⟶ U`, `h : W ⟶ V`,
708	restricting along `h ≫ g` is restricting along `g` then along `h`. -/
709	lemma restrictionMap_comp {U V W : C} (g : V ⟶ U) (h : W ⟶ V)
710	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
711	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) :
712	    restrictionMap (h ≫ g) φ = restrictionMap h (restrictionMap g φ) := by
713	  ext1 X
714	  exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (by rw [Over.mapComp_eq]; rfl))
715	
716	/-- **`restrictionMap` respects composition of morphisms** (functoriality of
717	`pushforward₀.map`). -/
718	lemma restrictionMap_comp_hom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
719	    {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
720	    (φ : restr U M ⟶ restr U N) (ψ : restr U N ⟶ restr U P) :
721	    restrictionMap g (φ ≫ ψ) = restrictionMap g φ ≫ restrictionMap g ψ :=
722	  Functor.map_comp _ _ _
723	
724	/-- **Restriction commutes with the global-scalar endomorphism.** For `g : V ⟶ U`
725	and a global scalar `r ∈ R(U)`, restricting the multiplication-by-`r` endomorphism of
726	`N|_U` yields the multiplication-by-`R(g)(r)` endomorphism of `N|_V`. This is the heart
727	of the semilinearity of `restrictionMap` (blueprint `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`):
728	both sides act on each section by scalar multiplication, and the scalars agree by
729	functoriality of `R` (`termRingMap_naturality`). -/
730	lemma restrictionMap_globalSMul {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
731	    {N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
732	    (r : (R.obj (Opposite.op U) : Type u)) :
733	    restrictionMap g (globalSMul (R := (Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 U))
734	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr U N) r)
735	      = globalSMul (R := (Over.forget V).op ⋙ R) (T := Over.mk (𝟙 V))
736	        Over.mkIdTerminal (restr V N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g.op)) r) := by
737	  ext1 Y
738	  ext m
739	  rw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
740	  erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
741	  congr 1
742	  simp only [termRingMap, Functor.comp_map]
743	  have hmor : (Over.forget U).op.map
744	        (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).op.obj Y).unop).op
745	      = g.op ≫ (Over.forget V).op.map (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op := by
746	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
747	    change Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
748	      = Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) ≫ g
749	    have e1 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop))
750	        = ((Over.map g).obj Y.unop).hom := by simp
751	    have e2 : Over.Hom.left (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop) = Y.unop.hom := by simp
752	    rw [e1, e2]
753	    simp [Over.map_obj_hom]
754	  rw [hmor]
755	  erw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← R.map_comp]
756	  rfl
757	
758	/-- **`restrictionMap` packaged as an additive group homomorphism** on the morphism
759	groups, using `restrictionMap_zero` and `restrictionMap_add`. This is the value of the
760	underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism of `internalHom`. -/
761	noncomputable def restrictionMapAddHom {U V : C} (g : V ⟶ U)
762	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
763	    (restr U M ⟶ restr U N) →+ (restr V M ⟶ restr V N) where
764	  toFun := restrictionMap g
765	  map_zero' := restrictionMap_zero g
766	  map_add' := restrictionMap_add g
767	
768	/-- **The underlying `Ab`-valued presheaf of `internalHom M N`**: `U ↦ (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)`
769	with the further-restriction maps. Functoriality is `restrictionMap_id` /
770	`restrictionMap_comp`. This is piece (a)+(b)+(c) of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
771	at the abelian-group level; the `R(U)`-module refinement is added by `internalHom`. -/
772	noncomputable def internalHomPresheaf
773	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
774	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{max u uC vC} where
775	  obj X := AddCommGrpCat.of (restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N)
776	  map {X Y} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (restrictionMapAddHom f.unop M N)
777	  map_id X := by
778	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
779	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
780	    exact restrictionMap_id φ
781	  map_comp {X Y Z} f f' := by
782	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
783	    refine AddMonoidHom.ext fun φ => ?_
784	    exact restrictionMap_comp f.unop f'.unop φ
785	
786	/-- **Semilinearity of `restrictionMap`** in the form consumed by
787	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`: for `f : X ⟶ Y` in `Cᵒᵖ`, a global scalar
788	`r ∈ R(X)` and `m : M|_{X} ⟶ N|_{X}`, `restrictionMap f.unop (r • m) =
789	R(f)(r) • restrictionMap f.unop m`, where the `•`'s are the slice-value module
790	actions (`internalHomObjModule`). This is `lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction`'s
791	compatibility datum; it follows from `restrictionMap_comp_hom` and
792	`restrictionMap_globalSMul`. -/
793	lemma restrictionMap_smul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
794	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
795	    (r : (R.obj X : Type u)) (m : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop N) :
796	    restrictionMap f.unop
797	        (letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop M N; r • m)
798	      = (letI := internalHomObjModule Y.unop M N;
799	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r • restrictionMap f.unop m) := by
800	  change restrictionMap f.unop (m ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr X.unop N) r)
801	    = restrictionMap f.unop m
802	        ≫ globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal (restr Y.unop N) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map f)) r)
803	  rw [restrictionMap_comp_hom, restrictionMap_globalSMul, Quiver.Hom.op_unop]
804	
805	end InternalHom
806	
807	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
808	
809	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
810	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
811	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
812	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
813	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
814	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
815	
816	namespace InternalHom
817	
818	section Assembly
819	
820	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
821	
822	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
823	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
824	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
825	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
826	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
827	section note for the universe constraint. -/
828	noncomputable def internalHom
829	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
830	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
831	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
832	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
833	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
834	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
835	
836	end Assembly
837	
838	end InternalHom
839	
840	namespace InternalHom
841	
842	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
843	
844	universe vC uC
845	
846	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
847	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
848	
849	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
850	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
851	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
852	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/
853	lemma termRingMap_terminal (f : (R.obj (op T) : Type u)) :
854	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT (op T))) f = f := by
855	  have hid : hT.from T = 𝟙 T := hT.hom_ext _ _
856	  simp only [termRingMap, unop_op, hid, op_id, R.map_id]
857	  rfl
858	
859	end InternalHom
860	
861	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
862	
863	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
864	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
865	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
866	
867	section Dual
868	
869	open InternalHom Opposite
870	
871	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
872	
873	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
874	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
875	over itself / the regular representation). The value
876	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
877	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
878	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
879	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
880	the varying structure sheaf. -/
881	noncomputable def dual
882	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
883	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
884	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
885	
886	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
887	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
888	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
889	noncomputable def evalLin
890	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
891	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
892	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
893	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
894	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
895	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
896	
897	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
898	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
899	lemma evalLin_add
900	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
901	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
902	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
903	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
904	
905	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
906	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
907	lemma evalLin_smul
908	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
909	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
910	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
911	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
912	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
913	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
914	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
915	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Stalk of a tensor product of presheaves of modules (ingredient d.2)
10	
11	This file constructs the *comparison map* underlying the varying-ring stalk--tensor
12	commutation isomorphism
13	
14	  `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x ≅ A.stalk x ⊗_{R.stalk x} B.stalk x`
15	
16	for presheaves of modules over the structure presheaf `R` of a scheme — the single
17	genuinely Mathlib-absent ingredient ("d.2") on which the unconditional associator of
18	the relative-Picard tensor substrate rests.
19	
20	This iteration builds the forward additive comparison map `stalkTensorDesc` and its
21	germ characterisation (the colimit-descent of the sectionwise base-change maps). The
22	full isomorphism `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorIso` (blueprint `lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`)
23	additionally requires the `R.stalk x`-linear packaging of this map, the reverse map, and
24	their mutual inversion on germs — see the handoff in `task_results`.
25	
26	The construction proceeds bottom-up:
27	
28	* `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorBilin` — for a neighbourhood `U ∋ x`, the `R(U)`-balanced
29	  bilinear map `A(U) × B(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, `(a,b) ↦ germ a ⊗ germ b`.
30	* `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDescU` — its descent through `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`
31	  to a map `(A ⊗ᵖ B)(U) = A(U) ⊗_{R(U)} B(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`.
32	* `PresheafOfModules.stalkTensorDesc` — the colimit descent to the comparison map
33	  `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`.
34	
35	Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma `lemma-stalk-tensor-product`.
36	
37	Blueprint: `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, §`sec:tensorobj_stalk_tensor`
38	(`lem:stalk_tensor_commutation`).
39	-/
40	
41	set_option autoImplicit false
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
46	
47	namespace PresheafOfModules
48	
49	open PresheafOfModules.Monoidal
50	
51	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — stalk of a tensor product (d.2) -/
52	
53	section StalkTensor
54	
55	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
56	  (A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) (x : X)
57	
58	local notation3 "Tgt" =>
59	  TensorProduct ↑(R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk A.presheaf x)
60	    ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x)
61	
62	/-- **The `R(U)`-balanced bilinear comparison map.** For a neighbourhood `U ∋ x`, the
63	bilinear (additive-monoid-hom) map `A(U) × B(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` sending
64	`(a, b)` to `germ a ⊗ germ b`. Project-local: the building block of the d.2 stalk--tensor
65	comparison; Mathlib has no varying-ring stalk--tensor commutation. -/
66	noncomputable def stalkTensorBilin (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
67	    (↑(A.obj (op U)) : Type u) →+ (↑(B.obj (op U)) : Type u) →+ Tgt where
68	  toFun m :=
69	    ((TensorProduct.mk ↑(R.stalk x) _ _
70	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) m)).toAddMonoidHom).comp
71	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx))
72	  map_zero' := by ext n; simp
73	  map_add' m m' := by ext n; simp
74	
75	/-- The defining balancing identity making `stalkTensorBilin` descend through the
76	relative tensor product `A(U) ⊗_{R(U)} B(U)`: `(r • a) ⊗ b ↦ a ⊗ (r • b)`. -/
77	lemma stalkTensorBilin_balanced (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (r : ↑(R.obj (op U)))
78	    (m : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (n : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
79	    (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx (r • m)) n = (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx m) (r • n) := by
80	  simp only [stalkTensorBilin, AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp,
81	    LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, Function.comp_apply, TensorProduct.mk_apply]
82	  rw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul A x U hx]
83	  erw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul B x U hx]
84	  exact TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _
85	
86	/-- **The per-neighbourhood descended comparison map** `(A ⊗ᵖ B)(U) → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`,
87	obtained from `stalkTensorBilin` via `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. The source object is
88	`(A ⊗ᵖ B).presheaf.obj (op U)`, definitionally `A(U) ⊗_{R(U)} B(U)`. -/
89	noncomputable def stalkTensorDescU (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
90	    (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.obj (op U) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of Tgt :=
91	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
92	    (TensorProduct.liftAddHom (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx) (stalkTensorBilin_balanced A B x U hx))
93	
94	@[simp] lemma stalkTensorDescU_tmul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
95	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
96	    (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b)
97	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
98	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b := by
99	  change (TensorProduct.liftAddHom (stalkTensorBilin A B x U hx)
100	    (stalkTensorBilin_balanced A B x U hx)) (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b) = _
101	  rw [TensorProduct.liftAddHom_tmul]
102	  rfl
103	
104	/-- **Scalar compatibility of the per-neighbourhood descent.** For `r : R(U)` and a
105	section `z` of `A ⊗ᵖ B` over `U`, the descent `stalkTensorDescU` carries the `R(U)`-scalar
106	action over to the `R.stalk x`-action by the germ of `r`. This is stage (iii)'s
107	section-level core; the carrier-duality between the `CommRingCat` scalar `r : R(U)` and the
108	`RingCat`-carrier module structure on `A(U) ⊗ B(U)` is absorbed by `germ_smul`/`smul_tmul'`. -/
109	lemma stalkTensorDescU_smul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (r : ↑(R.obj (op U)))
110	    (z : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).obj (op U))) :
111	    (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) (r • z)
112	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ R U x hx)) r •
113	          (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) z := by
114	  -- The section tensor `A(U) ⊗ B(U)` is a module over the `RingCat` carrier
115	  -- `S := (R ⋙ forget₂).obj (op U)`, which is defeq to the `CommRingCat` carrier `R(U)`.
116	  -- We pin `smul_tmul'`/`smul_add`/`smul_zero` to that ring so the scalar `r` slots in.
117	  induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
118	  | zero =>
119	      simp only [presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj, CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj,
120	        Monoidal.tensorObj_obj, map_zero, smul_zero]
121	      exact map_zero _
122	  | tmul a b =>
123	      erw [stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul, PresheafOfModules.germ_smul A x U hx]
124	      exact (TensorProduct.smul_tmul' _ _ _).symm
125	  | add p q hp hq =>
126	      erw [smul_add]
127	      refine (map_add _ _ _).trans ?_
128	      rw [hp, hq, ← smul_add]
129	      exact congrArg _ (map_add _ p q).symm
130	
131	/-- **The d.2 comparison map** `(A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x → A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x`, obtained by descending
132	the per-neighbourhood maps `stalkTensorDescU` through the colimit defining the stalk. -/
133	noncomputable def stalkTensorDesc :
134	    TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of Tgt :=
135	  colimit.desc ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ⋙ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf)
136	    ⟨AddCommGrpCat.of Tgt,
137	      fun U => stalkTensorDescU A B x (unop U).1 (unop U).2,
138	      by
139	        intro U V i
140	        apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
141	        ext z
142	        induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
143	        | zero => exact (AddMonoidHom.map_zero _).trans (AddMonoidHom.map_zero _).symm
144	        | tmul a b =>
145	            simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]
146	            erw [tensorObj_map_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul, stalkTensorDescU_tmul]
147	            erw [germ_res_apply, germ_res_apply]
148	            rfl
149	        | add p q hp hq =>
150	            exact (AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q).trans
151	              ((congrArg₂ HAdd.hAdd hp hq).trans (AddMonoidHom.map_add _ p q).symm)⟩
152	
153	/-- The comparison map `stalkTensorDesc` factors the per-neighbourhood map
154	`stalkTensorDescU` through the germ, by the colimit universal property. -/
155	@[reassoc]
156	lemma germ_stalkTensorDesc (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) :
157	    TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx ≫ stalkTensorDesc A B x
158	      = stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx :=
159	  colimit.ι_desc _ (op (⟨U, hx⟩ : OpenNhds x))
160	
161	/-- **Germ characterisation of the d.2 comparison map** on a simple tensor: the germ of
162	`a ⊗ b` over `U` maps to `germ a ⊗ germ b`. -/
163	@[simp]
164	lemma stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
165	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
166	    (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
167	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx))
168	          (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b))
169	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
170	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b := by
171	  rw [← stalkTensorDescU_tmul, ← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_stalkTensorDesc]
172	
173	/-- **Element form of `germ_stalkTensorDesc`.** The comparison map sends the germ of a
174	section `w` over `U` to its per-neighbourhood descent `stalkTensorDescU`. -/
175	lemma stalkTensorDesc_germ (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
176	    (w : ↑((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.obj (op U))) :
177	    (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
178	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx)) w)
179	      = (stalkTensorDescU A B x U hx) w := by
180	  rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_stalkTensorDesc]
181	
182	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear d.2 comparison map** (stage (iii)). The additive comparison
183	map `stalkTensorDesc` is `R.stalk x`-linear for the stalk module structures of
184	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`: linearity is checked on a germ `germ r` of a
185	scalar `r : R(U)` via the germ--scalar compatibility `germ_smul` and the section-level
186	scalar compatibility `stalkTensorDescU_smul`, after passing to a common neighbourhood.
187	This is the linear packaging the d.2 stalk--tensor isomorphism `stalkTensorIso` builds on. -/
188	noncomputable def stalkTensorLinearMap :
189	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x) : Type u)
190	      →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)] Tgt where
191	  toFun := ConcreteCategory.hom (stalkTensorDesc A B x)
192	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
193	  map_smul' r ξ := by
194	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
195	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
196	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, z, rfl⟩ :=
197	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x ξ
198	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
199	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
200	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
201	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
202	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
203	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
204	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
205	    have hz : (ConcreteCategory.hom
206	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf V x hxV)) z
207	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom
208	            (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf W x hxW))
209	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf.map iWV.op)) z) :=
210	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf iWV x hxW z).symm
211	    rw [hr, hz, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul (Monoidal.tensorObj A B) x W hxW,
212	      stalkTensorDesc_germ, stalkTensorDescU_smul, ← stalkTensorDesc_germ]
213	
214	/-- **Germ characterisation of the linear comparison map** on a simple tensor: the germ of
215	`a ⊗ b` over `U` maps to `germ a ⊗ germ b`. The `R.stalk x`-linear repackaging
216	`stalkTensorLinearMap` agrees with `stalkTensorDesc` on germs. -/
217	@[simp]
218	lemma stalkTensorLinearMap_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U)
219	    (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) (b : ↑(B.obj (op U))) :
220	    stalkTensorLinearMap A B x
221	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf U x hx))
222	          (a ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op U)] b))
223	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hx)) a ⊗ₜ[↑(R.stalk x)]
224	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf U x hx)) b :=
225	  stalkTensorDesc_germ_tmul A B x U hx a b
226	
227	/-! ### Stage (iv): the reverse map -/
228	
229	/-- **Inner cocone leg for the reverse map** (fixed `A`-section `a` over `U`). For a
230	`B`-neighbourhood `V ∋ x`, the additive map `B(V) → (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x` sending `b` to the
231	germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. Project-local building block of the d.2 reverse map. -/
232	private noncomputable def revInnerLeg (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
233	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) :
234	    B.presheaf.obj (op V) ⟶ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x :=
235	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom <|
236	    (ConcreteCategory.hom
237	        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩)).comp
238	      (((TensorProduct.mk ↑(R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))) ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V))) ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))
239	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
240	            ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))).toAddMonoidHom).comp
241	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)))
242	
243	/-- Evaluation of `revInnerLeg`: it sends `b` to the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
244	private lemma revInnerLeg_apply (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
245	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.presheaf.obj (op V))) :
246	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (revInnerLeg A B x U hxU a V hxV)) b
247	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
248	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
249	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
250	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
251	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
252	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) :=
253	  rfl
254	
255	/-- **Inner descent of the reverse map** (fixed `A`-section `a` over `U`): the additive
256	map `B.stalk x → (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x` descending `revInnerLeg` through the `B`-stalk colimit. -/
257	private noncomputable def revInner (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U))) :
258	    TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x ⟶
259	      TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x :=
260	  colimit.desc ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ⋙ B.presheaf)
261	    ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x,
262	      fun W => revInnerLeg A B x U hxU a (unop W).1 (unop W).2,
263	      by
264	        intro W W' f
265	        apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
266	        ext b
267	        simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]
268	        erw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.id_apply, revInnerLeg_apply]
269	        conv_rhs => erw [revInnerLeg_apply]
270	        have hVle : (unop W').1 ≤ (unop W).1 := leOfHom ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).map f.unop)
271	        have j : U ⊓ (unop W').1 ⟶ U ⊓ (unop W).1 := homOfLE (inf_le_inf_left U hVle)
272	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf j x
273	          ⟨hxU, (unop W').2⟩]
274	        congr 1
275	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
276	        congr 1
277	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe, ← presheaf_map_apply_coe]
278	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
279	          congr 1
280	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe, Functor.comp_map]
281	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
282	            ← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
283	          congr 1⟩
284	
285	/-- The inner descent factors `revInnerLeg` through the `B`-germ, by the colimit
286	universal property. -/
287	private lemma germ_revInner (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
288	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) :
289	    TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV ≫ revInner A B x U hxU a
290	      = revInnerLeg A B x U hxU a V hxV :=
291	  colimit.ι_desc _ (op (⟨V, hxV⟩ : OpenNhds x))
292	
293	/-- **Germ characterisation of the inner descent** on a section `b` over `V`: the inner
294	descent sends `germ b` to the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
295	private lemma revInner_germ (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
296	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.presheaf.obj (op V))) :
297	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (revInner A B x U hxU a))
298	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV)) b)
299	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
300	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
301	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
302	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
303	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
304	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) := by
305	  rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_revInner]
306	  exact revInnerLeg_apply A B x U hxU a V hxV b
307	
308	/-- **Outer cocone leg for the reverse map.** For a neighbourhood `U ∋ x`, the additive map
309	`A(U) → (B.stalk x →+ (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x)` sending an `A`-section `a` to the inner descent
310	`revInner` it determines. Additivity in `a` is checked on germ generators of `B.stalk x`
311	via `revInner_germ` and bilinearity of the section tensor. -/
312	private noncomputable def revOuterLeg (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
313	    (↑(A.obj (op U)) : Type u) →+
314	      ((↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x) : Type u) →+
315	        ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x)) where
316	  toFun a := ConcreteCategory.hom (revInner A B x U hxU a)
317	  map_add' a a' := by
318	    apply AddMonoidHom.ext
319	    intro ξ
320	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x ξ
321	    simp only [AddMonoidHom.add_apply, revInner_germ]
322	    erw [_root_.map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul, _root_.map_add]
323	  map_zero' := by
324	    apply AddMonoidHom.ext
325	    intro ξ
326	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x ξ
327	    simp only [AddMonoidHom.zero_apply, revInner_germ]
328	    erw [_root_.map_zero, TensorProduct.zero_tmul, _root_.map_zero]
329	
330	/-- Evaluation of the outer leg: it is the inner descent. -/
331	private lemma revOuterLeg_apply (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
332	    (ξ : ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x)) :
333	    (revOuterLeg A B x U hxU a) ξ = (ConcreteCategory.hom (revInner A B x U hxU a)) ξ :=
334	  rfl
335	
336	/-- **Outer descent of the reverse map.** The additive map
337	`A.stalk x → (B.stalk x →+ (A ⊗ᵖ B).stalk x)` descending `revOuterLeg` through the
338	`A`-stalk colimit; this is the additive bilinear comparison underlying the reverse map. -/
339	private noncomputable def revBihom :
340	    TopCat.Presheaf.stalk A.presheaf x ⟶
341	      AddCommGrpCat.of ((↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk B.presheaf x) : Type u) →+
342	        ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf x)) :=
343	  colimit.desc ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).op ⋙ A.presheaf)
344	    ⟨AddCommGrpCat.of _,
345	      fun U => AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (revOuterLeg A B x (unop U).1 (unop U).2),
346	      by
347	        intro W W' f
348	        apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
349	        ext a
350	        simp only [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, Functor.const_obj_map]
351	        erw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.id_apply]
352	        apply AddMonoidHom.ext
353	        intro ξ
354	        obtain ⟨V, hxV, b, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist B.presheaf x ξ
355	        erw [revOuterLeg_apply, revOuterLeg_apply, revInner_germ, revInner_germ]
356	        have hVle : (unop W').1 ≤ (unop W).1 := leOfHom ((OpenNhds.inclusion x).map f.unop)
357	        have j : (unop W').1 ⊓ V ⟶ (unop W).1 ⊓ V := homOfLE (inf_le_inf_right V hVle)
358	        rw [← TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf j x
359	          ⟨(unop W').2, hxV⟩]
360	        congr 1
361	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
362	        congr 1
363	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe, ← presheaf_map_apply_coe, Functor.comp_map]
364	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
365	            ← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
366	          congr 1
367	        · rw [← presheaf_map_apply_coe]
368	          erw [← CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp]
369	          congr 1⟩
370	
371	/-- The outer descent factors `revOuterLeg` through the `A`-germ. -/
372	private lemma germ_revBihom (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
373	    TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU ≫ revBihom A B x
374	      = AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (revOuterLeg A B x U hxU) :=
375	  colimit.ι_desc _ (op (⟨U, hxU⟩ : OpenNhds x))
376	
377	/-- **Germ characterisation of the additive bilinear comparison** on germ generators:
378	`revBihom (germ a) (germ b)` is the germ of `(a|_{U⊓V}) ⊗ (b|_{U⊓V})`. -/
379	private lemma revBihom_germ_tmul (U : Opens X) (hxU : x ∈ U) (a : ↑(A.obj (op U)))
380	    (V : Opens X) (hxV : x ∈ V) (b : ↑(B.obj (op V))) :
381	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x))
382	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU)) a))
383	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ B.presheaf V x hxV)) b)
384	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
385	          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (Monoidal.tensorObj A B).presheaf (U ⊓ V) x ⟨hxU, hxV⟩))
386	          (((ConcreteCategory.hom (A.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_left : U ⊓ V ⟶ U).op)) a :
387	              ↑(A.obj (op (U ⊓ V)))) ⊗ₜ[R.obj (op (U ⊓ V))]
388	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (B.presheaf.map (homOfLE inf_le_right : U ⊓ V ⟶ V).op)) b :
389	              ↑(B.obj (op (U ⊓ V))))) := by
390	  have hb : (ConcreteCategory.hom (revBihom A B x))
391	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ A.presheaf U x hxU)) a)
392	      = revOuterLeg A B x U hxU a := by
393	    rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, germ_revBihom]
394	    rfl
395	  rw [hb, revOuterLeg_apply, revInner_germ]
396	
397	/-- **The `W ⊓ W` section identity underlying the stage-(iv) balancing.** Over the
398	self-intersection `W ⊓ W`, restricting a scalar-twisted simple tensor commutes between the

1052	          (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ
1053	      map_add' := fun φ ψ => Preadditive.comp_add _ _ _ _ φ ψ
1054	      map_smul' := fun r φ => by
1055	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1056	        exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
1057	      left_inv := fun φ => by
1058	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1059	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv
1060	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1061	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom ≫ φ) = φ
1062	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.inv_hom_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1063	          Category.id_comp]
1064	      right_inv := fun ψ => by
1065	        change (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1066	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.hom
1067	          ≫ ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U)
1068	            (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).map e.inv ≫ ψ) = ψ
1069	        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, e.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
1070	          Category.id_comp] }
1071	
1072	/-- **The presheaf dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
1073	`e : M ≅ M'` of presheaves of modules induces an isomorphism `dual M' ≅ dual M` of their
1074	duals (`dual N = internalHom N (𝟙_)`), assembled sectionwise from the precomposition
1075	equivalences `dualPrecompEquiv`. This is the `\leanok`-ready ingredient that transports a
1076	local trivialisation `L|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` to `dual (L|_U) ≅ dual 𝒪_U` in the assembly of
1077	`dual_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1078	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso
1079	    {M M' : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)} (e : M ≅ M') :
1080	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
1081	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk
1082	    (fun U => (dualPrecompEquiv e U.unop).toModuleIso)
1083	
1084	end Dual
1085	
1086	end PresheafOfModules

154	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
155	
156	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
157	
158	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
159	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
160	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
161	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
162	
163	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
164	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
165	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
166	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
167	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
168	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
169	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
170	
171	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
172	
173	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
174	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
175	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
176	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
177	
178	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
179	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
180	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
181	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
182	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
183	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
184	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
185	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
186	
187	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
188	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
189	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
190	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
191	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
192	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
193	law is definitional. -/
194	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
195	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
196	
197	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
198	
199	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
200	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the

685	`homOfLocalCompat` (SheafOfModules morphism descent). The B-bridge

690	lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
691	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
692	    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧

694	  -- iter-226 descent re-route (d.2-FREE). `Linv := Scheme.Modules.dual L` is now

702	  --       presheaf `dual` (= `internalHom(-, R)`) across the open-immersion ring iso.
703	  --   (A) SheafOfModules morphism descent: glue the canonical local trivialising isos

714	/-! ## §5. The invertibility-carrier Picard group `picCommGroup`
715	
716	This is the by-hand commutative-group law on isomorphism classes of `⊗`-invertible
717	`𝒪_X`-modules (blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`). Every group axiom is a single
718	existence-of-isomorphism `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` read as an equality of iso-classes; no
719	pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence and no `MonoidalCategory` instance is invoked.
720	The inverse is carried by the membership witness of `IsInvertible` itself. -/
721	
722	/-- **Step 1 — associator on `⊗`-invertible objects** (blueprint
723	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`). An immediate specialisation of the now
724	*unconditional* `tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the invertibility hypotheses are not consumed
725	(they match the blueprint statement). -/
726	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
727	    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
728	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
729	  tensorObj_assoc_iso
730	
731	/-- **Middle-four interchange for `⊗_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `𝒪_X`-modules
732	`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`,
733	assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
734	Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
735	private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95
